translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And king David was old [and] advanced in age; and they covered him with clothes, but he obtained no warmth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And his servants said to him, Let there be found for my lord the king a young virgin; and let her stand before the king, and let her cherish him, and let her lie in thy bosom, that my lord the king may get warm.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they sought for a fair damsel throughout the territory of Israel, and found Abishag a Shunammite, and brought her to the king.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the damsel was very fair; and cherished the king, and ministered to him; but the king knew her not.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Adonijah the son of Haggith exalted himself, saying, I will be king; and he provided himself chariots and horsemen, and fifty men to run before him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And his father had not grieved him at any time in saying, Why doest thou so? and he was also a very comely man; and [his mother] bore him after Absalom.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he conferred with Joab the son of Zeruiah, and with Abiathar the priest; and they helped Adonijah and followed [him].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Zadok the priest, and Benaiah the son of Jehoiada, and Nathan the prophet, and Shimei, and Rei, and the mighty men whom David had, were not with Adonijah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Adonijah sacrificed sheep and oxen and fatted cattle by the stone of Zoheleth, which is by En-rogel, and invited all his brethren, the king's sons, and all the men of Judah, the king's servants;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but Nathan the prophet, and Benaiah, and the mighty men, and Solomon his brother, he did not invite.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Nathan spoke to Bathsheba the mother of Solomon, saying, Hast thou not heard that Adonijah the son of Haggith is king, and David our lord does not know [it]?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And now, come, let me, I pray thee, give thee counsel, that thou mayest save thine own life, and the life of thy son Solomon.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Go and get thee in to king David, and say to him, Didst not thou, my lord, O king, swear to thy handmaid saying, Solomon thy son shall reign after me, and he shall sit on my throne? why then does Adonijah reign?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Behold, while thou yet talkest there with the king, I also will come in after thee and confirm thy words.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Bathsheba went in to the king into the chamber; and the king was very old; and Abishag the Shunammite ministered to the king.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Bathsheba bowed and did obeisance to the king. And the king said, What wouldest thou?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And she said to him, My lord, thou hast sworn by Jehovah thyGod to thy handmaid, [saying,] Solomon thy son shall reign after me, and he shall sit on my throne.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And now behold, Adonijah is king; and now, my lord the king, thou knowest it not.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he has sacrificed oxen and fatted cattle and sheep in abundance, and has invited all the sons of the king and Abiathar the priest and Joab the captain of the host; but Solomon thy servant has he not invited.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou, my lord the king, the eyes of all Israel are upon thee, that thou shouldest tell them who shall sit on the throne of my lord the king after him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Otherwise it shall come to pass when my lord the king shall sleep with his fathers, that I and my son Solomon will be counted offenders.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And behold, while she yet talked with the king, Nathan the prophet also came in.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they told the king saying, Behold, Nathan the prophet. And when he was come in before the king, he bowed himself before the king with his face to the ground.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Nathan said, My lord, O king, hast thou said, Adonijah shall reign after me, and he shall sit on my throne?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For he is gone down this day, and has sacrificed oxen and fatted cattle and sheep in abundance, and has invited all the king's sons, and the captains of the host, and Abiathar the priest; and behold, they eat and drink before him, and say,God save king Adonijah!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But me, [even] me thy servant, and Zadok the priest, and Benaiah the son of Jehoiada, and thy servant Solomon, he has not invited.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Is this thing done by my lord the king, and thou hast not shewn to thy servants who should sit on the throne of my lord the king after him?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then king David answered and said, Call me Bathsheba. And she came into the king's presence, and stood before the king.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the king swore, and said, [As] Jehovah liveth, who has redeemed my soul out of all distress,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: even as I swore to thee by Jehovah theGod of Israel, saying, Solomon thy son shall reign after me, and he shall sit on my throne in my stead; even so will I certainly do this day.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Bathsheba bowed with her face to the earth, and did reverence to the king, and said, Let my lord king David live for ever.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And king David said, Call me Zadok the priest, and Nathan the prophet, and Benaiah the son of Jehoiada. And they came before the king.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the king said to them, Take with you the servants of your lord, and cause Solomon my son to ride upon mine own mule, and bring him down to Gihon;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and let Zadok the priest and Nathan the prophet anoint him there king over Israel; and blow ye with the trumpet, and say, Long live king Solomon!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And ye shall come up after him, that he may come and sit on my throne; for he shall reign in my stead, and I have appointed him to be ruler over Israel and over Judah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Benaiah the son of Jehoiada answered the king and said, Amen: Jehovah, theGod of my lord the king, say so too.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: As Jehovah has been with my lord the king, even so be he with Solomon, and make his throne greater than the throne of my lord king David.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Zadok the priest, and Nathan the prophet, and Benaiah the son of Jehoiada, and the Cherethites and the Pelethites went down, and caused Solomon to ride upon king David's mule, and brought him to Gihon.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Zadok the priest took the horn of oil out of the tabernacle, and anointed Solomon. And they blew the trumpet; and all the people said, Long live king Solomon!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And all the people came up after him, and the people piped with pipes, and rejoiced with great joy, so that the earth rent with the sound of them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Adonijah and all the guests that were with him heard [it] as they were finishing their repast; and Joab heard the sound of the trumpet, and he said, Wherefore this noise of the city in an uproar?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: While he yet spoke, behold, Jonathan the son of Abiathar the priest came; and Adonijah said [to him], Come in; for thou art a valiant man, and bringest good news.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jonathan answered and said to Adonijah, Indeed, our lord king David has made Solomon king.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the king has sent with him Zadok the priest, and Nathan the prophet, and Benaiah the son of Jehoiada, and the Cherethites and the Pelethites, and they have caused him to ride upon the king's mule;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and Zadok the priest and Nathan the prophet have anointed him king at Gihon; and they are come up from thence rejoicing, so that the city is in an uproar. This is the noise which ye have heard.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And also Solomon is sitting on the throne of the kingdom.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And moreover the king's servants came to bless our lord king David, saying, ThyGod make the name of Solomon more excellent than thy name, and make his throne greater than thy throne! And the king bowed himself on the bed.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And also thus said the king: Blessed be Jehovah, theGod of Israel, who has given one to sit on my throne this day, mine eyes even seeing it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And all Adonijah's guests were afraid, and rose up and went every man his way.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Adonijah feared because of Solomon, and arose and went and caught hold of the horns of the altar.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it was told Solomon saying, Behold, Adonijah fears king Solomon; for behold, he has caught hold of the horns of the altar, saying, Let king Solomon swear to me this day that he will not put his servant to death with the sword.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Solomon said, If he be a worthy man, there shall not one of his hairs fall to the earth; but if wickedness be found in him, he shall die.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And king Solomon sent, and they brought him down from the altar. And he came and bowed himself to king Solomon; and Solomon said to him, Go to thy house.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the days of David were at hand that he should die; and he enjoined Solomon his son saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I go the way of all the earth: be of good courage therefore, and be a man;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and keep the charge of Jehovah thyGod, to walk in his ways, to keep his statutes, and his commandments, and his ordinances, and his testimonies, as it is written in the law of Moses, that thou mayest prosper in all that thou doest and whithersoever thou turnest thyself;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: that Jehovah may confirm his word which he spoke concerning me, saying, If thy sons take heed to their way, to walk before me in truth with all their heart and with all their soul, there shall not fail thee, said he, a man upon the throne of Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou knowest also what Joab the son of Zeruiah did to me, what he did to the two captains of the hosts of Israel, to Abner the son of Ner, and to Amasa the son of Jether, whom he slew, and shed the blood of war in peace, and put the blood of war upon his girdle that was about his loins, and upon his sandals that were on his feet.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt do according to thy wisdom, and not let his hoar head go down to Sheol in peace.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But shew kindness to the sons of Barzillai the Gileadite, and let them be of those that eat at thy table; for so they came up to me when I fled because of Absalom thy brother.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And behold, there is with thee Shimei the son of Gera, the Benjaminite of Bahurim, who cursed me with a grievous curse in the day that I went to Mahanaim; but he came down to meet me at the Jordan, and I swore to him by Jehovah saying, I will not put thee to death with the sword.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And now hold him not guiltless; for thou art a wise man, and thou shalt know what thou oughtest to do to him; but bring his hoar head down to Sheol with blood.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And David slept with his fathers, and was buried in the city of David.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the days that David reigned over Israel were forty years: he reigned seven years in Hebron, and he reigned thirty-three years in Jerusalem.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Solomon sat on the throne of David his father; and his kingdom was established greatly.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Adonijah the son of Haggith came to Bathsheba the mother of Solomon. And she said, Comest thou peaceably? And he said, Peaceably.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said, I have something to say to thee. And she said, Speak.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said, Thou knowest that the kingdom was mine, and all Israel had set their faces on me that I should reign; but the kingdom is turned about and is become my brother's, for it was his from Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And now I ask one petition of thee; refuse me not. And she said to him, Speak.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said, Speak, I pray thee, to Solomon the king — for he will not refuse thee — that he give me Abishag the Shunammite as wife.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Bathsheba said, Well, I will speak for thee to the king.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Bathsheba went to king Solomon, to speak to him for Adonijah. And the king rose up to meet her, and bowed himself to her, and sat down on his throne; and he caused a throne to be set for the king's mother, and she sat on his right hand.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then she said, I desire one small petition of thee; refuse me not. And the king said to her, Ask, my mother, for I will not refuse thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And she said, Let Abishag the Shunammite be given to Adonijah thy brother as wife.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And king Solomon answered and said to his mother, And why dost thou ask Abishag the Shunammite for Adonijah? ask for him the kingdom also; for he is mine elder brother; even for him, and for Abiathar the priest, and for Joab the son of Zeruiah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And king Solomon swore by Jehovah saying,God do so to me, and more also, — Adonijah has spoken this word against his own life!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And now [as] Jehovah liveth, who has established me, and set me on the throne of David my father, and who has made me a house, as he promised, Adonijah shall be put to death this day.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And king Solomon sent by the hand of Benaiah the son of Jehoiada; who fell on him, that he died.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the king said to Abiathar the priest, Go to Anathoth, to thine own fields; for thou art worthy of death; but I will not at this time put thee to death, because thou didst bear the ark of Adonai Jehovah before David my father, and because thou hast been afflicted in all wherein my father was afflicted.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Solomon thrust out Abiathar from being priest to Jehovah, to fulfil the word of Jehovah, which he had spoken concerning the house of Eli in Shiloh.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the report came to Joab (for Joab had turned after Adonijah, though he had not turned after Absalom); and Joab fled to the tent of Jehovah, and caught hold of the horns of the altar.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it was told king Solomon that Joab had fled to the tent of Jehovah; and behold, he is by the altar. And Solomon sent Benaiah the son of Jehoiada, saying, Go, fall on him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Benaiah came to the tent of Jehovah and said to him, Thus saith the king: Come forth. And he said, No; for I will die here. And Benaiah brought the king word again, saying, Thus said Joab, and thus he answered me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the king said to him, Do as he has said, and fall upon him, and bury him; and take away the innocent blood, which Joab shed, from me and from the house of my father.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah shall requite the blood which he shed upon his own head, because he fell upon two men more righteous and better than he, and slew them with the sword, without my father David's knowledge: Abner the son of Ner, captain of the host of Israel, and Amasa the son of Jether, captain of the host of Judah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And their blood shall be requited upon the head of Joab, and upon the head of his seed for ever; but upon David, and upon his seed, and upon his house, and upon his throne, shall there be peace for ever from Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Benaiah the son of Jehoiada went up and fell upon him, and put him to death; and he was buried in his own house in the wilderness.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the king put Benaiah the son of Jehoiada in his stead over the host; and Zadok the priest the king put in the stead of Abiathar.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the king sent and called for Shimei, and said to him, Build thee a house in Jerusalem, and abide there, and go not forth thence anywhere.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it shall be that on the day thou goest forth, and passest over the torrent of Kidron, ...know for certain that thou shalt surely die: thy blood shall be upon thine own head.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Shimei said to the king, The saying is good: as my lord the king has said, so will thy servant do. And Shimei dwelt in Jerusalem many days.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass at the end of three years, that two servants of Shimei's ran away to Achish son of Maachah, king of Gath. And they told Shimei saying, Behold, thy servants are in Gath.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then Shimei arose, and saddled his ass, and went to Gath, to Achish, to seek his servants; and Shimei went, and brought his servants from Gath.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it was told Solomon that Shimei had gone from Jerusalem to Gath, and had come again.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the king sent and called for Shimei, and said to him, Did I not make thee swear by Jehovah, and protest to thee, saying, Know for certain, that on the day thou goest forth, and walkest abroad anywhere, thou shalt surely die? and thou saidst to me, The word that I have heard is good.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Why then hast thou not kept the oath of Jehovah, and the commandment that I charged thee with?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the king said to Shimei, Thou knowest all the wickedness of which thy heart is conscious, which thou didst to David my father; and Jehovah returns thy wickedness upon thine own head;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and king Solomon shall be blessed, and the throne of David shall be established before Jehovah for ever.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the king commanded Benaiah the son of Jehoiada; who went out and fell upon him, and he died. And the kingdom was established in the hand of Solomon.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Solomon allied himself by marriage with Pharaoh king of Egypt, and took Pharaoh's daughter, and brought her into the city of David, until he had ended building his own house, and the house of Jehovah, and the wall of Jerusalem round about.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Only, the people sacrificed on the high places; for there was no house built to the name of Jehovah, until those days.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Solomon loved Jehovah, walking in the statutes of David his father; only, he sacrificed and burned incense on the high places.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the king went to Gibeon to sacrifice there; for that was the great high place: a thousand burnt-offerings did Solomon offer up upon that altar.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: In Gibeon Jehovah appeared to Solomon in a dream by night; andGod said, Ask what I shall give thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Solomon said, Thou hast shewn unto thy servant David my father great loving-kindness, according as he walked before thee in truth, and in righteousness, and in uprightness of heart with thee; and thou hast kept for him this great loving-kindness, that thou hast given him a son who sits upon his throne, as it is this day.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And now, Jehovah myGod, thou hast made thy servant king instead of David my father; and I am but a little child: I know not to go out and to come in.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thy servant is in the midst of thy people which thou hast chosen, a great people, that cannot be numbered nor counted for multitude.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Give therefore to thy servant an understanding heart, to judge thy people, to discern between good and bad; for who is able to judge this thy numerous people?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the word pleased the Lord, that Solomon had asked this thing.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: AndGod said to him, Because thou hast asked this thing, and hast not asked for thyself long life, neither hast asked riches for thyself, nor hast asked the life of thine enemies, but hast asked for thyself discernment to understand judgment;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: behold, I have done according to thy word: behold, I have given thee a wise and an understanding heart, so that there hath been none like unto thee before thee, neither after thee shall any arise like unto thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I have also given thee that which thou hast not asked, both riches and glory; so that there shall not be any among the kings like unto thee all thy days.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And if thou wilt walk in my ways, to keep my statutes and my commandments, as thy father David did walk, then I will prolong thy days.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Solomon awoke, and behold, it was a dream. And he came to Jerusalem, and stood before the ark of the covenant of Jehovah, and offered up burnt-offerings, and offered peace-offerings, and made a feast to all his servants.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then came two women, harlots, to the king, and stood before him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the first woman said, Ah, my lord! I and this woman abode in one house; and I was delivered of a child with her in the house.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass the third day after I was delivered, that this woman was delivered also; and we were together; no stranger was with us in the house, only we two were in the house.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And this woman's child died in the night; because she had lain upon it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And she arose at midnight, and took my son from beside me, while thy handmaid slept, and laid it in her bosom, and laid her dead son in my bosom.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when I rose in the morning to give my child suck, behold, it was dead; and I considered it in the morning, and behold, it was not my son, whom I bore.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the other woman said, No, for the living is my son, and the dead is thy son. And this one said, No, but the dead is thy son, and the living is my son. Thus they spoke before the king.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then said the king, The one says, This that is living is my son, and thy son is the dead; and the other says, No, for thy son is the dead, and my son is the living.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the king said, Bring me a sword. And they brought a sword before the king.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the king said, Divide the living child in two, and give half to the one, and half to the other.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then spoke the woman whose was the living child to the king, for her bowels yearned over her son, and she said, Ah, my lord! give her the living child, and in no wise put it to death. But the other said, Let it be neither mine nor thine; divide it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the king answered and said, Give this one the living child, and in no wise put it to death: she is its mother.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And all Israel heard of the judgment which the king had judged; and they feared the king, for they saw that the wisdom ofGod was in him, to do justice.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And king Solomon was king over all Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And these are the princes whom he had: Azariah the son of Zadok was priest;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Elihoreph and Ahiah, the sons of Shisha, scribes; Jehoshaphat the son of Ahilud, chronicler;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and Benaiah the son of Jehoiada was over the host; and Zadok and Abiathar were priests;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and Azariah the son of Nathan was over the superintendents; and Zabud the son of Nathan was principal officer, the king's friend;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and Ahishar was over the household; and Adoniram the son of Abda was over the levy-service.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Solomon had twelve superintendents over all Israel; and they provided food for the king and his household: each man his month in the year had to make provision.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And these are their names: Ben-Hur, in mount Ephraim.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Ben-Deker in Makaz, and in Shaalbim, and Beth-shemesh, and Elon-Beth-hanan.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Ben-Hesed, in Arubboth; he had Sochoh, and all the land of Hepher.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Ben-Abinadab had all the upland of Dor; Taphath the daughter of Solomon was his wife.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Baana the son of Ahilud had Taanach and Megiddo, and all Beth-shean, which is by Zaretan beneath Jizreel, from Beth-shean to Abel-Meholah, as far as beyond Jokneam.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Ben-Geber, in Ramoth-Gilead; he had the villages of Jair the son of Manasseh, which are in Gilead; he had the region of Argob, which is in Bashan, sixty great cities with walls and bars of bronze.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Ahinadab the son of Iddo, at Mahanaim.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Ahimaaz, in Nephtali; he also took Basmath the daughter of Solomon as wife.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Baanah the son of Hushai, in Asher and in Aloth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehoshaphat the son of Paruah, in Issachar.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Shimei the son of Ela, in Benjamin.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Geber the son of Uri, in the land of Gilead, the land of Sihon the king of the Amorites, and of Og the king of Bashan; and [he was] the only superintendent that was in the land.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Judah and Israel were many, as the sand which is by the sea in multitude, eating and drinking and making merry.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Solomon ruled over all kingdoms from the river to the land of the Philistines, and as far as the border of Egypt: they brought presents, and served Solomon all the days of his life.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Solomon's provision for one day was thirty measures of fine flour, and sixty measures of meal,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: ten fatted oxen, and twenty oxen out of the pastures, and a hundred sheep, besides harts, and gazelles, and fallow-deer, and fatted fowl.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For he had dominion over all on this side the river, from Tiphsah as far as Gazah, over all the kings on this side the river; and he had peace on all sides round about.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Judah and Israel dwelt safely, every man under his vine and under his fig-tree, from Dan even to Beer-sheba, all the days of Solomon.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Solomon had forty thousand stalls of horses for his chariots, and twelve thousand horsemen.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And those officers provided food for king Solomon, and for all who came to king Solomon's table, every man in his month: they let nothing be wanting.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the barley, and the straw for the horses and coursers, they brought to the place where [the superintendents] were, every man according to his charge.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: AndGod gave Solomon wisdom and very great understanding and largeness of heart, even as the sand that is on the sea-shore.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Solomon's wisdom excelled the wisdom of all the sons of the east, and all the wisdom of Egypt.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For he was wiser than all men; than Ethan the Ezrahite, and Heman, and Calcol, and Darda, the sons of Mahol; and his fame was in all the nations round about.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he spoke three thousand proverbs; and his songs were a thousand and five.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he spoke of the trees, from the cedar-tree that is on Lebanon even to the hyssop that springs out of the wall; he spoke also of cattle, and of fowls, and of creeping things, and of fishes.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And there came of all peoples to hear the wisdom of Solomon, from all the kings of the earth who had heard of his wisdom.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Hiram king of Tyre sent his servants to Solomon; for he had heard that they had anointed him king in the place of his father; for Hiram always loved David.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Solomon sent to Hiram, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou knowest that David my father could not build a house unto the name of Jehovah hisGod, because of the wars which were about him on every side, until Jehovah put them under the soles of his feet.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But now Jehovah myGod has given me rest on every side: there is neither adversary nor evil event.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And behold, I purpose to build a house unto the name of Jehovah myGod, as Jehovah spoke to David my father saying, Thy son, whom I will set upon thy throne in thy stead, he shall build a house unto my name.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And now command that they hew me cedar-trees out of Lebanon; and my servants shall be with thy servants; and I will give thee hire for thy servants according to all that thou shalt say; for thou knowest that there is not among us any that are experienced in cutting timber like to the Zidonians.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass when Hiram heard the words of Solomon, that he rejoiced greatly, and said, Blessed be Jehovah this day, who has given to David a wise son over this great people.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Hiram sent to Solomon, saying, I have heard the things which thou sentest to me for: I will do all thy desire concerning timber of cedar, and concerning timber of cypress.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: My servants shall bring [them] down from Lebanon to the sea; and I will convey them by sea [in] rafts to the place that thou shalt appoint me, and will cause them to be broken up there, and thou shalt receive them. And thou shalt accomplish my desire in giving food for my household.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: So Hiram gave Solomon cedar-trees and cypress-trees [according to] all his desire.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Solomon gave Hiram twenty thousand measures of wheat as food for his household, and twenty measures of beaten oil: thus gave Solomon to Hiram year by year.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah gave Solomon wisdom as he promised him. And there was peace between Hiram and Solomon; and they two made a league.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And king Solomon raised a levy out of all Israel; and the levy was thirty thousand men.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he sent them to Lebanon, ten thousand a month by courses; a month they were in Lebanon, two months at home; and Adoniram was over the levy.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Solomon had seventy thousand that bore burdens, and eighty thousand stone-masons in the mountains;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: besides the overseers whom Solomon had set over the work, three thousand three hundred, who ruled over the people that wrought in the work.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the king commanded, and they brought great stones, costly stones, hewn stones, to lay the foundation of the house.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Solomon's builders and Hiram's builders and the Giblites hewed them, and prepared timber and stones to build the house.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass in the four hundred and eightieth year after the children of Israel were come out of the land of Egypt, in the fourth year of Solomon's reign over Israel, in the month Zif, which is the second month, that he began to build the house of Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the house that king Solomon built for Jehovah was sixty cubits in length, and twenty in breadth, and thirty cubits in height.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the porch, in front of the temple of the house, was twenty cubits in length, in front of the house broadways, [and] ten cubits was its breadth, in front of the house.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And for the house he made closed windows with fixed lattices.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And against the wall of the house he built floors round about, [against] the walls of the house, round about the temple and the oracle; and he made side-chambers round about.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The lowest floor was five cubits broad, and the middle one was six cubits broad, and the third was seven cubits broad; for in the [thickness of the wall of] the house he made resets round about outside, that nothing should be fastened in the walls of the house.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the house, when it was being built, was built of stone entirely made ready before it was brought thither; so that there was neither hammer nor axe nor any tool of iron heard in the house while it was being built.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The entrance to the side-chambers of the middle [floor] was in the right side of the house; and they went up by winding stairs into the middle [floor], and out of the middle into the third.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he built the house, and finished it; and covered the house with beams and boards of cedar.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he built the floors against all the house, five cubits high; and they held to the house by the timbers of cedar.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the word of Jehovah came to Solomon saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: As to this house which thou art building, if thou wilt walk in my statutes, and practise mine ordinances, and keep all my commandments to walk in them, then will I perform my word as to thee which I spoke unto David thy father;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and I will dwell among the children of Israel, and will not forsake my people Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Solomon built the house and finished it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he built the walls of the house within with boards of cedar, from the floor of the house to the walls of the roof; he overlaid [them] on the inside with wood, and overlaid the floor of the house with boards of cypress.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he built twenty cubits of the innermost part of the house, both floor and walls, with boards of cedar; and he built [them] for it within, to [be] the oracle, the most holy place.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the house, that is, the temple before it, was forty cubits [long].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the cedar of the house within was carved with colocynths and half-open flowers: all was cedar; there was no stone seen.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he prepared the oracle in the midst of the house within, to set there the ark of the covenant of Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the oracle within was twenty cubits in length, and twenty cubits in breadth, and twenty cubits in the height thereof; and he overlaid it with pure gold; and he overlaid the cedar-wood altar —

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and Solomon overlaid the house within with pure gold, and shut off the oracle in front with chains of gold, and overlaid it with gold.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the whole house he overlaid with gold, the whole house entirely; also the whole altar that was by the oracle he overlaid with gold.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he made in the oracle two cherubim of olive-wood, ten cubits high;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and one wing of the cherub was five cubits, and five cubits the other wing of the cherub, ten cubits from the end of one wing to the end of the other wing;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and the other cherub was ten cubits: both the cherubim were of one measure and one form.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The height of the one cherub was ten cubits, and so of the other cherub.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he set the cherubim in the midst of the inner house; and the wings of the cherubim were stretched forth, so that the wing of the one touched the wall, and the wing of the other cherub touched the other wall; and their wings touched, wing to wing, in the midst of the house.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he overlaid the cherubim with gold.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he carved all the walls of the house round about with carved sculptures of cherubim, and palm-trees, and half-open flowers, within and without.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the floor of the house he overlaid with gold, within and without.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And for the entrance of the oracle he made doors of olive-wood: the lintel [and] side posts were the fifth part [of the breadth of the house].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The two doors were of olive-wood; and he carved on them carvings of cherubim, and palm-trees and half-open flowers, and overlaid them with gold, and spread gold on the cherubim and on the palm-trees.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he also made for the doorway of the temple posts of olive-wood, of the fourth part [of the breadth of the house].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the two folding-doors were of cypress-wood: the two leaves of the one door were folding, and the two leaves of the other door were folding.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he carved on them cherubim, and palm-trees, and half-open flowers; and overlaid them with gold fitted on the carved work.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he built the inner court of three rows of hewn stone, and a row of cedar-beams.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: In the fourth year was the foundation of the house of Jehovah laid, in the month Zif;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and in the eleventh year, in the month Bul, which is the eighth month, was the house finished in all its parts, and according to all the fashion of it. So he was seven years in building it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Solomon was thirteen years building his own house; and he finished all his house.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he built the house of the forest of Lebanon; its length was a hundred cubits, and its breadth fifty cubits, and its height thirty cubits, upon four rows of cedar-pillars, with cedar-beams upon the pillars;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and it was covered with cedar above upon the side-chambers, which were on forty-five pillars, fifteen in a row.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And there were cross-beams in three rows, and window was against window in three ranks.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And all the doors and posts were square, with an architrave; and window was against window in three ranks.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he made the porch of pillars; its length was fifty cubits, and its breadth thirty cubits; and there was a porch in front of them; and there were pillars, and steps in front of them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he made the porch for the throne where he judged, the porch of judgment; and it was covered with cedar from floor to floor.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And his house where he dwelt had another court within the porch, which was of the like work. And he made, like to this porch, a house for Pharaoh's daughter, whom Solomon had taken.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: All these [buildings] were of costly stones, hewn stones, according to the measures, sawed with saws, within and without, even from the foundation to the coping, and on the outside as far as the great court.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the foundations were of costly stones, great stones, stones of ten cubits and stones of eight cubits.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And above were costly stones, hewn stones, according to the measures, and cedar.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the great court round about had three rows of hewn stones, and a row of cedar-beams; and so it was for the inner court of the house of Jehovah, and the porch of the house.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And king Solomon sent and fetched Hiram out of Tyre.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He was a widow's son of the tribe of Naphtali, and his father was a man of Tyre, a worker in brass; and he was full of wisdom and understanding and knowledge, to do all kinds of works in brass. And he came to king Solomon, and made all his work.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he formed the two pillars of brass; the height of one pillar was eighteen cubits, and a line of twelve cubits encompassed the second pillar.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he made two capitals of molten brass, to set upon the tops of the pillars; the height of the one capital was five cubits, and the height of the other capital was five cubits;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: [and] nets of checker-work, wreaths of chain-work, for the capitals which were upon the top of the pillars; seven for the one capital and seven for the other capital.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he made pomegranates, namely two rows round about upon the one network, to cover the capitals that were upon the top of the pillars; and so he did for the other capital.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the capitals that were upon the top of the pillars were of lily-work [as] in the porch, four cubits.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the capitals upon the two pillars, above also, close to the enlargement which was behind the network, had two hundred pomegranates in rows round about, [also] on the other capital.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he set up the pillars for the porch of the temple; and he set up the right pillar, and called its name Jachin; and he set up the left pillar, and called its name Boaz.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And upon the top of the pillars was lily-work; and the work of the pillars was finished.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he made the sea, molten, ten cubits from brim to brim, round all about; and its height was five cubits; and a line of thirty cubits encompassed it round about.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And under the brim of it round about there were colocynths, encompassing it, ten in a cubit enclosing the sea round about; two rows of colocynths, cast when it was cast.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: It stood upon twelve oxen, three looking toward the north, and three looking toward the west, and three looking toward the south, and three looking toward the east; and the sea was above upon them, and all their hinder parts were inward.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And its thickness was a hand-breadth, and its brim was like the work of the brim of a cup, with lily-blossoms; it held two thousand baths.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he made ten bases of brass: four cubits was the length of one base, and the breadth four cubits, and the height three cubits.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the work of the bases was this: they had panels, and the panels were between the fillets.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And on the panels that were between the fillets were lions, oxen and cherubim; and over the fillets there was a base above; and beneath the lions and oxen were garlands of festoon-work.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And every base had four wheels of brass, and axles of brass; and on its four corners were shoulder-pieces: under the laver were shoulder-pieces molten, behind every garland.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the mouth of it within the crown and above was a cubit; and its mouth was rounded, [as] the work of the base, a cubit and a half; and also upon its mouth was sculpture; but their panels were square, not round.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the four wheels were under the panels; and the supports of the wheels were in the base; and the height of a wheel was a cubit and half a cubit.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the work of the wheels was like the work of a chariot wheel: their supports, and their rims, and their spokes and their naves were all molten.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And there were four shoulder-pieces to the four corners of one base; of the base itself were its shoulder-pieces.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And in the top of the base there was a circular elevation of half a cubit round about; and on the top of the base its stays and its panels were of the same.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he engraved on the plates of its stays and on its panels cherubim, lions and palm-trees, according to the space upon each; and garlands were round about.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: After this [manner] he made the ten bases: all of them had one casting, one measure, one form.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he made ten lavers of brass: one laver contained forty baths; every laver was four cubits; upon every one of the ten bases one laver.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he put the bases, five on the right side of the house, and five on the left side of the house; and he set the sea on the right side of the house eastward, over against the south.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Hiram made the lavers, and the shovels, and the bowls. So Hiram ended doing all the work that he made for king Solomon [for] the house of Jehovah:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: two pillars, and the globes of the capitals that were on the top of the pillars, two; and the two networks, to cover the two globes of the capitals which were on the top of the pillars;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and the four hundred pomegranates for the two networks, two rows of pomegranates for one network, to cover the two globes of the capitals which were upon the pillars;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and the ten bases, and the ten lavers on the bases;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and one sea, and the twelve oxen under the sea;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and the pots, and the shovels, and the bowls. And all these things, which Hiram made king Solomon for the house of Jehovah, were of bright brass.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: In the plain of the Jordan did the king cast them, in the clay-ground between Succoth and Zaretan.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Solomon left all the vessels [unweighed] from their exceeding number; the weight of the brass was not ascertained.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Solomon made all the vessels that were [in] the house of Jehovah: the golden altar; and the table of gold, whereon was the shewbread;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and the candlesticks of pure gold, five on the right, and five on the left, before the oracle; and the flowers, and the lamps, and the tongs of gold,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and the basons, and the knives, and the bowls, and the cups, and the censers of pure gold; and the hinges of gold, for the folding-doors of the inner house, the most holy place, [and] for the doors of the house, of the temple.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And all the work was finished that king Solomon made for the house of Jehovah. And Solomon brought in the things that David his father had dedicated; the silver and the gold and the vessels he put among the treasures of the house of Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then Solomon assembled the elders of Israel, and all the heads of the tribes, the princes of the fathers of the children of Israel, unto king Solomon in Jerusalem, to bring up the ark of the covenant of Jehovah out of the city of David, which is Zion.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And all the men of Israel assembled themselves to king Solomon at the feast in the month Ethanim, that is, the seventh month.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And all the elders of Israel came; and the priests took up the ark.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they brought up the ark of Jehovah, and the tent of meeting, and all the holy vessels that were in the tent: the priests and the Levites brought them up.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And king Solomon, and all the assembly of Israel that were assembled to him, [who were] with him before the ark, sacrificed sheep and oxen, which could not be counted nor numbered for multitude.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the priests brought in the ark of the covenant of Jehovah to its place, into the oracle of the house, into the most holy place, under the wings of the cherubim;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for the cherubim stretched forth [their] wings over the place of the ark, and the cherubim covered the ark and its staves above.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the staves were long, so that the ends of the staves were seen from the holy place before the oracle, but they were not seen without. And there they are to this day.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: There was nothing in the ark save the two tables of stone which Moses placed there at Horeb, when Jehovah made [a covenant] with the children of Israel, when they came out of the land of Egypt.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass when the priests were come out of the holy place, that the cloud filled the house of Jehovah,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and the priests could not stand to do their service because of the cloud; for the glory of Jehovah had filled the house of Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then said Solomon: Jehovah said that he would dwell in the thick darkness.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I have indeed built a house of habitation for thee, a settled place for thee to abide in for ever.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the king turned his face, and blessed the whole congregation of Israel; and the whole congregation of Israel stood.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said: Blessed be Jehovah theGod of Israel, who spoke with his mouth unto David my father, and hath with his hand fulfilled it, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Since the day that I brought forth my people Israel out of Egypt, I chose no city out of all the tribes of Israel to build a house in, that my name might be there; but I have chosen David to be over my people Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it was in the heart of David my father to build a house unto the name of Jehovah theGod of Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Jehovah said to David my father, Whereas it was in thy heart to build a house unto my name, thou didst well that it was in thy heart;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: nevertheless thou shalt not build the house; but thy son that shall come forth out of thy loins, he shall build the house unto my name.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah has performed his word which he spoke; and I am risen up in the room of David my father, and sit on the throne of Israel, as Jehovah promised, and I have built the house unto the name of Jehovah theGod of Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I have set there a place for the ark, wherein is the covenant of Jehovah, which he made with our fathers when he brought them out of the land of Egypt.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Solomon stood before the altar of Jehovah in the presence of the whole congregation of Israel, and spread forth his hands toward the heavens.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said, Jehovah,God of Israel! there is noGod like thee, in the heavens above, or on the earth beneath, who keepest covenant and mercy with thy servants that walk before thee with all their heart;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: who hast kept with thy servant David my father that which thou didst promise him; thou spokest with thy mouth, and hast fulfilled [it] with thy hand, as at this day.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And now, Jehovah,God of Israel, keep with thy servant David my father that which thou hast promised him, saying, There shall not fail thee a man in my sight to sit on the throne of Israel, if only thy sons take heed to their way, to walk before me as thou hast walked before me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And now, OGod of Israel, let thy words, I pray thee, be verified, which thou hast spoken unto thy servant David my father.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But willGod indeed dwell on the earth? Behold, the heavens, and the heaven of heavens, cannot contain thee; how much less this house which I have built!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Yet have respect unto the prayer of thy servant, and to his supplication, Jehovah, myGod, to hearken unto the cry and to the prayer which thy servant prayeth before thee this day;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: that thine eyes may be open upon this house night and day, upon the place of which thou hast said, My name shall be there: to hearken unto the prayer which thy servant prayeth toward this place.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And hearken unto the supplication of thy servant, and of thy people Israel, when they shall pray toward this place, and hear thou in thy dwelling-place, in the heavens, and when thou hearest, forgive.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If a man have sinned against his neighbour, and an oath be laid upon him to adjure him, and the oath come before thine altar in this house;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: then hear thou in the heavens, and do, and judge thy servants, condemning the wicked, to bring his way upon his own head; and justifying the righteous, giving him according to his righteousness.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: When thy people Israel are put to the worse before the enemy, because they have sinned against thee, and shall turn again to thee, and confess thy name, and pray, and make supplication unto thee in this house;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: then hear thou in the heavens, and forgive the sin of thy people Israel, and bring them again unto the land that thou gavest unto their fathers.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: When the heavens are shut up, and there is no rain, because they have sinned against thee; if they pray toward this place, and confess thy name, and turn from their sin, because thou hast afflicted them;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: then hear thou in the heavens, and forgive the sin of thy servants, and of thy people Israel, when thou teachest them the good way wherein they should walk; and give rain upon thy land, which thou hast given to thy people for an inheritance.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If there be famine in the land, if there be pestilence, if there be blight, mildew, locust, caterpillar; if their enemy besiege them in the land of their gates; whatever plague, whatever sickness there be:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: what prayer, what supplication soever be made by any man, of all thy people Israel, when they shall know every man the plague of his own heart, and shall spread forth his hands toward this house;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: then hear thou in the heavens, the settled place of thy dwelling, and forgive, and do, and render unto every man according to all his ways, whose heart thou knowest (for thou, thou only, knowest the hearts of all the children of men),

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: that they may fear thee all the days that they live upon the land which thou gavest unto our fathers.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And as to the stranger also, who is not of thy people Israel, but cometh out of a far country for thy name's sake

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: (for they shall hear of thy great name, and of thy mighty hand, and of thy stretched-out arm); when he shall come and pray toward this house,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: hear thou in the heavens thy dwelling-place, and do according to all that the stranger calleth to thee for; in order that all peoples of the earth may know thy name, [and] that they may fear thee as do thy people Israel; and that they may know that this house which I have built is called by thy name.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If thy people go out to battle against their enemy, by the way that thou shalt send them, and they pray to Jehovah toward the city that thou hast chosen, and the house that I have built unto thy name;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: then hear thou in the heavens their prayer and their supplication, and maintain their right.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If they have sinned against thee, (for there is no man that sinneth not,) and thou be angry with them, and give them up to the enemy, and they have carried them away captives unto the enemy's land, far or near;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and if they shall take it to heart in the land whither they were carried captive, and repent, and make supplication unto thee in the land of them that carried them captive, saying, We have sinned, and have done iniquity, we have dealt perversely;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and if they return unto thee with all their heart and with all their soul, in the land of their enemies who led them away captive, and pray unto thee toward their land which thou gavest unto their fathers, the city that thou hast chosen, and the house that I have built unto thy name;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: then hear thou in the heavens, the settled place of thy dwelling, their prayer and their supplication, and maintain their right;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and forgive thy people their sin against thee, and all their transgressions whereby they have transgressed against thee, and give them to find compassion with those who carried them captive, that they may have compassion on them

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: (for they are thy people, and thine inheritance, which thou broughtest forth out of Egypt, from the midst of the furnace of iron) —

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: thine eyes being open unto the supplication of thy servant, and unto the supplication of thy people Israel, to hearken unto them in all that they call for unto thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For thou hast separated them from among all peoples of the earth, to be thine inheritance, as thou spokest through Moses thy servant, when thou broughtest our fathers out of Egypt, O Lord Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it was so, that when Solomon had ended praying all this prayer and supplication to Jehovah, he arose from before the altar of Jehovah, from kneeling on his knees with his hands spread forth to the heavens,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and he stood and blessed the whole congregation of Israel with a loud voice, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Blessed be Jehovah, who has given rest to his people Israel, according to all that he promised: there has not failed one word of all his good promises which he spoke through Moses his servant!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah ourGod be with us, as he was with our fathers; let him not forsake us nor cast us off:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: that he may incline our hearts to him, to walk in all his ways, and to keep his commandments and his statutes and his ordinances, which he commanded our fathers.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And let these my words, with which I have made supplication before Jehovah, be nigh to Jehovah ourGod day and night, that he maintain the right of his servant, and the right of his people Israel, as the matter of each day shall require;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: that all peoples of the earth may know that Jehovah isGod, that there is none else;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and that your heart may be perfect with Jehovah ourGod, to walk in his statutes and to keep his commandments, as at this day.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the king, and all Israel with him, offered sacrifices before Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Solomon sacrificed a sacrifice of peace-offerings, which he sacrificed to Jehovah, twenty-two thousand oxen and a hundred and twenty thousand sheep. So the king and all the children of Israel dedicated the house of Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The same day the king hallowed the middle of the court that was before the house of Jehovah; for there he offered the burnt-offerings, and the oblations, and the fat of the peace-offerings, because the brazen altar that was before Jehovah was too small to receive the burnt-offerings, and the oblations, and the fat of the peace-offerings.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And at that time Solomon held the feast, and all Israel with him, a great congregation, from the entrance of Hamath unto the torrent of Egypt, before Jehovah ourGod, seven days and seven days, fourteen days.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: On the eighth day he sent the people away; and they blessed the king, and went to their tents, joyful and glad of heart for all the goodness that Jehovah had done to David his servant, and to Israel his people.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass when Solomon had completed the building of the house of Jehovah, and the king's house, and all Solomon's desire which he was pleased to do,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: that Jehovah appeared to Solomon the second time, as he had appeared to him at Gibeon.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah said to him, I have heard thy prayer and thy supplication which thou hast made before me: I have hallowed this house, which thou hast built, to put my name there for ever; and mine eyes and my heart shall be there perpetually.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And [as for] thee, if thou wilt walk before me, as David thy father walked, in integrity of heart, and in uprightness, to do according to all that I have commanded thee, [and] wilt keep my statutes and mine ordinances;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: then will I establish the throne of thy kingdom over Israel for ever, as I promised to David thy father, saying, There shall not fail thee a man upon the throne of Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: [But] if ye shall at all turn from following me, ye or your children, and will not keep my commandments, my statutes which I have set before you, but go and serve othergods, and worship them;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: then will I cut off Israel out of the land which I have given them; and the house, which I have hallowed to my name, will I cast out of my sight; and Israel shall be a proverb and a by word among all peoples;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and this house, [which] is high, every one that passes by it shall be astonished at, and shall hiss, and they shall say, Why has Jehovah done thus to this land and to this house?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they shall say, Because they forsook Jehovah theirGod, who brought forth their fathers out of the land of Egypt, and have attached themselves to othergods, and have worshipped them and served them; therefore has Jehovah brought upon them all this evil.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass at the end of twenty years, when Solomon had built the two houses, the house of Jehovah and the king's house,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Hiram the king of Tyre having furnished Solomon with cedar-trees and cypress-trees, and with gold, according to all his desire, — that then king Solomon gave Hiram twenty cities in the land of Galilee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Hiram came out from Tyre to see the cities that Solomon had given him; and they did not please him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said, What cities are these which thou hast given me, my brother? And he called them the land of Cabul to this day.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Hiram had sent to the king one hundred and twenty talents of gold.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And this is the account of the levy which king Solomon raised, to build the house of Jehovah, and his own house, and Millo, and the wall of Jerusalem, and Hazor, and Megiddo, and Gezer.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — Pharaoh king of Egypt had gone up and taken Gezer, and burned it with fire, and slain the Canaanites that dwelt in the city, and given it as a dowry to his daughter, Solomon's wife.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — And Solomon built Gezer, and lower Beth-horon,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and Baalath, and Tadmor in the wilderness, in the land,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and all the store-cities that Solomon had, and cities for chariots, and cities for the horsemen, and that which Solomon desired to build in Jerusalem, and on Lebanon, and in all the land of his dominion.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: All the people that were left of the Amorites, the Hittites, the Perizzites, the Hivites, and the Jebusites, who were not of the children of Israel,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: their children that were left after them in the land, whom the children of Israel were not able utterly to destroy, upon them did Solomon impose a tribute of bondservice until this day.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But of the children of Israel did Solomon make no bondmen; but they were men of war, and his servants, and his chiefs, and his captains, and captains of his chariots, and his horsemen.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: These were the chief superintendents that were over Solomon's work, five hundred and fifty, that ruled over the people that wrought in the work.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Pharaoh's daughter came up out of the city of David to her house which he had built for her: then he built Millo.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And three times in the year did Solomon offer up burnt-offerings and peace-offerings upon the altar that he had built to Jehovah, and he burned incense upon that which was before Jehovah. So he finished the house.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And king Solomon made a fleet of ships in Ezion-Geber, which is beside Eloth, on the shore of the Red Sea, in the land of Edom.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Hiram sent in the fleet his servants, shipmen that had knowledge of the sea, with the servants of Solomon;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and they went to Ophir, and fetched thence gold, four hundred and twenty talents, and brought it to king Solomon.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the queen of Sheba heard of the fame of Solomon in connection with the name of Jehovah, and came to prove him with enigmas.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And she came to Jerusalem with a very great train, with camels that bore spices and gold in very great abundance, and precious stones; and she came to Solomon, and spoke to him of all that was in her heart.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Solomon explained to her all she spoke of: there was not a thing hidden from the king that he did not explain to her.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when the queen of Sheba saw all Solomon's wisdom, and the house that he had built,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and the food of his table, and the deportment of his servants, and the order of service of his attendants, and their apparel, and his cupbearers, and his ascent by which he went up to the house of Jehovah, there was no more spirit in her.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And she said to the king, It was a true report that I heard in mine own land of thine affairs, and of thy wisdom;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but I gave no credit to the words, until I came and mine eyes had seen; and behold, the half was not told me: in wisdom and prosperity thou exceedest the report that I heard.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Happy are thy men! happy are these thy servants, who stand continually before thee, who hear thy wisdom!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Blessed be Jehovah thyGod, who delighted in thee, to set thee on the throne of Israel! Because Jehovah loves Israel for ever, therefore did he make thee king, to do judgment and justice.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And she gave the king a hundred and twenty talents of gold, and spices in very great abundance, and precious stones: there came no more such abundance of spices as those which the queen of Sheba gave to king Solomon.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: (And the fleet also of Hiram, which carried gold from Ophir, brought from Ophir sandal-wood in very great abundance, and precious stones.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the king made of the sandal-wood a balustrade for the house of Jehovah, and for the king's house, and harps and lutes for the singers. There came no such sandal-wood, nor was there seen to this day.)

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And king Solomon gave to the queen of Sheba all her desire, whatever she asked, besides what he gave her of the bounty of king Solomon. And she turned and went to her own land, she and her servants.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the weight of gold that came to Solomon in one year was six hundred and sixty-six talents of gold,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: besides what [came] by the dealers, and by the traffic of the merchants, and by all the kings of Arabia, and by the governors of the country.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And king Solomon made two hundred targets of beaten gold, — he applied six hundred [shekels] of gold to one target;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and three hundred shields of beaten gold, — he applied three minas of gold to one shield; and the king put them in the house of the forest of Lebanon.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the king made a great throne of ivory, and overlaid it with refined gold:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: the throne had six steps, and the top of the throne was rounded behind; and there were arms on each side at the place of the seat, and two lions stood beside the arms;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and twelve lions stood there on the one side and on the other upon the six steps: there was not the like made in any kingdom.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And all king Solomon's drinking vessels were of gold, and all the vessels of the house of the forest of Lebanon were of precious gold: none were of silver, [which] was not of the least account in the days of Solomon.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For the king had on the sea a Tarshish-fleet, with the fleet of Hiram: once in three years came the Tarshish-fleet, bringing gold and silver, ivory, and apes, and peacocks.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And king Solomon was greater than all the kings of the earth in riches and in wisdom.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And all the earth sought the presence of Solomon, to hear his wisdom, whichGod had put in his heart.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they brought every man his present, vessels of silver, and vessels of gold, and clothing, and armour, and spices, horses and mules, a rate year by year.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Solomon gathered chariots and horsemen; and he had a thousand four hundred chariots, and twelve thousand horsemen; and he placed them in the chariot-cities, and with the king at Jerusalem.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the king made silver in Jerusalem as stones, and cedars made he as the sycamores that are in the lowland for abundance.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the exportation of horses that Solomon had was from Egypt: a caravan of the king's merchants fetched a drove [of horses], at a price.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And a chariot came up and went out of Egypt for six hundred [shekels] of silver, and a horse for a hundred and fifty; and so they brought [them] by their means, for all the kings of the Hittites and for the kings of Syria.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But king Solomon loved many foreign women, besides the daughter of Pharaoh: women of the Moabites, Ammonites, Edomites, Zidonians, Hittites;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: of the nations of which Jehovah had said to the children of Israel, Ye shall not go in to them, neither shall they come in to you; they would certainly turn away your heart after theirgods: to these Solomon was attached in love.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he had seven hundred wives, princesses, and three hundred concubines; and his wives turned away his heart.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass when Solomon was old, [that] his wives turned away his heart after othergods; and his heart was not perfect with Jehovah hisGod, as the heart of David his father.

translations.name: Darby Bible
