translations.text: And Solomon went after Ashtoreth thegoddess of the Zidonians, and after Milcom the abomination of the Ammonites.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Solomon did evil in the sight of Jehovah, and followed not fully Jehovah, as David his father.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then did Solomon build a high place for Chemosh the abomination of the Moabites, on the hill that is before Jerusalem, and for Molech the abomination of the children of Ammon.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And so he did for all his foreign wives, who burned incense and sacrificed to theirgods.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah was angry with Solomon, because his heart was turned away from Jehovah theGod of Israel, who had appeared to him twice,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and had commanded him concerning this thing, not to go after othergods; but he kept not what Jehovah had commanded.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah said to Solomon, Forasmuch as this is done by thee, and thou hast not kept my covenant and my statutes which I commanded thee, I will certainly rend the kingdom from thee, and will give it to thy servant:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: notwithstanding in thy days I will not do it, for David thy father's sake; I will rend it out of the hand of thy son;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: only, I will not rend away all the kingdom: I will give one tribe to thy son, for David my servant's sake, and for Jerusalem's sake which I have chosen.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah stirred up an adversary to Solomon, Hadad the Edomite; he was of the king's seed in Edom.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now it came to pass when David was in Edom, when Joab the captain of the host had gone up to bury the slain, after he had smitten every male in Edom

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: (for Joab abode there six months with all Israel, until he had cut off every male in Edom),

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: that Hadad fled, he and certain Edomites of his father's servants with him, to go into Egypt, Hadad being yet a little child.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they arose out of Midian, and came to Paran, and took men with them out of Paran, and they came to Egypt, to Pharaoh king of Egypt; who gave him a house, and appointed him victuals, and gave him land.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Hadad found great favour in the sight of Pharaoh, and he gave him as wife the sister of his own wife, the sister of Tahpenes the queen.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the sister of Tahpenes bore him Genubath his son; and Tahpenes brought him up in Pharaoh's house; and Genubath was in Pharaoh's household, among the sons of Pharaoh.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Hadad heard in Egypt that David slept with his fathers, and that Joab the captain of the host was dead; and Hadad said to Pharaoh, Let me depart, that I may go to mine own country.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Pharaoh said to him, What then dost thou lack with me, that behold, thou desirest to go to thine own country? And he said, Nothing; but in any case let me depart.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: God stirred him up yet an adversary, Rezon the son of Eliada, who had fled from Hadadezer king of Zobah, his lord.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he collected men to him, and became captain of a band, when David slew them [of Zobah]; and they went to Damascus, and dwelt there, and reigned in Damascus.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he was an adversary to Israel all the days of Solomon, besides the mischief that Hadad [did]; and he abhorred Israel, and reigned over Syria.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jeroboam the son of Nebat, an Ephrathite of Zeredah, Solomon's servant (whose mother's name was Zeruah, a widow woman), even he lifted up his hand against the king.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And this was the cause that he lifted up his hand against the king: Solomon was building Millo, and closing the breach of the city of David his father;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and the man Jeroboam was strong and valiant; and Solomon saw the young man that he was industrious, and he made him ruler over all the charge of the house of Joseph.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass at that time that Jeroboam went out of Jerusalem, and the prophet Ahijah the Shilonite found him in the way; and he had clad himself with a new garment; and they two were alone in the field.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then Ahijah seized the new garment that was on him, and rent it in twelve pieces;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and said to Jeroboam, Take thee ten pieces; for thus saith Jehovah theGod of Israel: Behold, I will rend the kingdom out of the hand of Solomon, and will give ten tribes to thee;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but one tribe shall he have for my servant David's sake, and for Jerusalem's sake, the city which I have chosen out of all the tribes of Israel:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: because they have forsaken me, and have worshipped Ashtoreth thegoddess of the Zidonians, Chemosh thegod of the Moabites, and Milcom thegod of the children of Ammon, and have not walked in my ways, to do that which is right in my sight, and my statutes and mine ordinances, as David his father.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But I will not take the whole kingdom out of his hand; for I will make him prince all the days of his life for David my servant's sake, whom I chose, who kept my commandments and my statutes;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but I will take the kingdom out of his son's hand, and will give it unto thee, — the ten tribes.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And unto his son will I give one tribe, that David my servant may have a lamp always before me in Jerusalem, the city that I have chosen for myself to put my name there.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I will take thee, that thou mayest reign over all that thy soul desireth, and thou shalt be king over Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it shall be, if thou wilt hearken unto all that I command thee, and wilt walk in my ways, and do that which is right in my sight, in keeping my statutes and my commandments, as David my servant did, that I will be with thee, and build thee a lasting house, as I built for David, and will give Israel unto thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I will for this afflict the seed of David, but not for ever.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Solomon sought to kill Jeroboam; and Jeroboam arose and fled into Egypt, to Shishak king of Egypt; and he was in Egypt until the death of Solomon.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the rest of the acts of Solomon, and all that he did, and his wisdom, are they not written in the book of the acts of Solomon?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the time that Solomon reigned in Jerusalem over all Israel was forty years.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Solomon slept with his fathers, and was buried in the city of David his father; and Rehoboam his son reigned in his stead.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Rehoboam went to Shechem; for all Israel had come to Shechem to make him king.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass when Jeroboam the son of Nebat heard it (now he was yet in Egypt, whither he had fled from the presence of king Solomon, and Jeroboam dwelt in Egypt;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and they sent and called him), that Jeroboam and all the congregation of Israel came and spoke to Rehoboam, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thy father made our yoke grievous; and now lighten thou the grievous servitude of thy father and his heavy yoke that he put upon us, and we will serve thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to them, Depart yet for three days, then come again to me. And the people departed.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And king Rehoboam consulted with the old men, who had stood before Solomon his father while he yet lived, saying, How do ye advise to return answer to this people?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they spoke to him saying, If this day thou wilt be a servant to this people, and wilt serve them and answer them and speak good words to them, they will be thy servants for ever.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But he forsook the advice of the old men which they had given him, and consulted with the young men, who had grown up with him, that stood before him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to them, What advice give ye that we may return answer to this people who have spoken to me saying, Lighten the yoke which thy father put upon us?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the young men that had grown up with him spoke to him saying, Thus shalt thou say to this people that have spoken to thee saying, Thy father made our yoke heavy, and lighten thou it for us, — thus shalt thou say to them: My little [finger] is thicker than my father's loins;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and whereas my father laid a heavy yoke upon you, I will add to your yoke: my father chastised you with whips, but I will chastise you with scorpions.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jeroboam and all the people came to Rehoboam the third day, as the king had appointed saying, Come again to me on the third day.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the king answered the people roughly, and forsook the advice of the old men which they had given him;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and he spoke to them according to the advice of the young men, saying, My father made your yoke heavy, but I will add to your yoke: my father chastised you with whips, but I will chastise you with scorpions.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: So the king hearkened not to the people; for it was brought about by Jehovah, that he might give effect to his word, which Jehovah spoke through Ahijah the Shilonite to Jeroboam the son of Nebat.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And all Israel saw that the king hearkened not to them; and the people answered the king saying, What portion have we in David? And [we have] no inheritance in the son of Jesse: To your tents, O Israel! Now see to thine own house, David! And Israel went to their tents.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But as for the children of Israel that dwelt in the cities of Judah, Rehoboam reigned over them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And king Rehoboam sent Adoram, who was over the levy; but all Israel stoned him with stones, that he died. And king Rehoboam hastened to mount his chariot, to flee to Jerusalem.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Israel rebelled against the house of David, unto this day.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass when all Israel heard that Jeroboam had come back, that they sent and called him to the assembly, and made him king over all Israel: none followed the house of David, but the tribe of Judah only.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Rehoboam came to Jerusalem; and he assembled all the house of Judah, with the tribe of Benjamin, a hundred and eighty thousand chosen men apt for war, to fight against the house of Israel, that he might bring the kingdom again to Rehoboam the son of Solomon.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But the word ofGod came to Shemaiah the man ofGod, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Speak unto Rehoboam, the son of Solomon, king of Judah, and unto all the house of Judah and Benjamin, and to the remainder of the people, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thus saith Jehovah: Go not up, nor fight with your brethren, the children of Israel; return every man to his house, for this thing is from me. And they hearkened to the word of Jehovah, and returned to depart, according to the word of Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jeroboam built Shechem in mount Ephraim, and dwelt therein; and went out from thence, and built Penuel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jeroboam said in his heart, Now shall the kingdom return to the house of David.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If this people go up to do sacrifice in the house of Jehovah at Jerusalem, the heart of this people will turn again to their lord, to Rehoboam king of Judah, and they will kill me, and return to Rehoboam king of Judah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the king took counsel, and made two calves of gold. And he said to them, It is too much for you to go up to Jerusalem: behold thygods, Israel, which brought thee up out of the land of Egypt!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he set the one in Bethel, and the other he put in Dan.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And this thing became a sin; and the people went [to worship] before the one, as far as Dan.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he made a house of high places, and made priests from all classes of the people, who were not of the sons of Levi.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jeroboam ordained a feast in the eighth month, on the fifteenth day of the month, like the feast that was in Judah, and he offered upon the altar. So did he in Bethel, sacrificing to the calves that he had made; and he placed in Bethel the priests of the high places that he had made.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he offered upon the altar that he had made in Bethel, on the fifteenth day of the eighth month, in the month which he had devised of his own heart; and he made a feast for the children of Israel, and he offered upon the altar, burning incense.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And behold, there came a man ofGod from Judah, by the word of Jehovah, to Bethel; and Jeroboam stood by the altar to burn incense.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he cried against the altar by the word of Jehovah, and said, O altar, altar! thus saith Jehovah: Behold, a child shall be born unto the house of David, Josiah by name; and upon thee shall he sacrifice the priests of the high places that burn incense upon thee, and men's bones shall be burned upon thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he gave a sign the same day, saying, This is the sign that Jehovah hath spoken: Behold, the altar shall be rent, and the ashes that are upon it shall be poured out.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass when the king heard the word of the man ofGod, which he cried against the altar in Bethel, that Jeroboam stretched forth his hand from the altar, saying, Lay hold on him. And his hand which he stretched out against him dried up, so that he could not bring it back again to him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the altar was rent, and the ashes poured out from the altar, according to the sign that the man ofGod had given by the word of Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the king answered and said to the man ofGod, Intreat now Jehovah thyGod, and pray for me, that my hand may be restored to me again. And the man ofGod intreated Jehovah, and the king's hand was restored to him again, and became as before.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the king said to the man ofGod, Come home with me, and refresh thyself, and I will give thee a present.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the man ofGod said to the king, If thou wilt give me half thy house, I will not go in with thee, neither will I eat bread nor drink water in this place;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for so was it charged me by the word of Jehovah, saying, Thou shalt eat no bread, nor drink water, nor return by the way that thou shalt go.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: So he went another way, and returned not by the way that he had come to Bethel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now there dwelt a certain old prophet in Bethel; and his sons came and told him all the works that the man ofGod had done that day in Bethel; the words that he had spoken to the king, them they told also to their father.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And their father said to them, Which way went he? for his sons had seen which way the man ofGod went, who came from Judah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to his sons, Saddle me the ass. So they saddled him the ass; and he rode thereon,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and went after the man ofGod, and found him sitting under a terebinth; and he said to him, Art thou the man ofGod that camest from Judah? And he said, I am.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to him, Come home with me, and eat bread.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said, I cannot return with thee, nor go in with thee; neither will I eat bread nor drink water with thee in this place.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For it was said to me by the word of Jehovah, Thou shalt eat no bread nor drink water there, nor turn again to go by the way that thou wentest.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to him, I am a prophet also as thou art; and an angel spoke to me by the word of Jehovah saying, Bring him back with thee into thy house, that he may eat bread and drink water. He lied unto him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then he went back with him, and ate bread in his house, and drank water.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass as they sat at the table, that the word of Jehovah came to the prophet that brought him back;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and he cried to the man ofGod that came from Judah, saying, Thus saith Jehovah: Forasmuch as thou hast disobeyed the word of Jehovah, and hast not kept the commandment that Jehovah thyGod commanded thee,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but camest back, and hast eaten bread and drunk water in the place of which he said to thee, Eat no bread and drink no water; thy carcase shall not come to the sepulchre of thy fathers.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass, after he had eaten bread, and after he had drunk, that he saddled the ass for him, for the prophet that he had brought back;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and he departed. And a lion met him by the way and slew him; and his corpse was cast in the way, and the ass stood by it; the lion also stood by the corpse.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And behold, men passed by, and saw the corpse cast in the way and the lion standing by the corpse; and they came and told it in the city where the old prophet dwelt.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the prophet that brought him back from the way heard [of it] and said, It is the man ofGod who was disobedient to the word of Jehovah; therefore Jehovah has delivered him to the lion, which has torn him and slain him, according to the word of Jehovah which he spoke to him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he spoke to his sons saying, Saddle me the ass; and they saddled it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he went and found his corpse cast in the way, and the ass and the lion standing by the corpse: the lion had not eaten the corpse, nor torn the ass.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the prophet took up the corpse of the man ofGod, and laid it upon the ass, and brought it back; and the old prophet came into the city, to mourn and to bury him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he laid his corpse in his own sepulchre; and they mourned over him [saying], Alas, my brother!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass after he had buried him, that he spoke to his sons saying, When I am dead, bury me in the sepulchre in which the man ofGod is buried; lay my bones beside his bones.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For the word that he cried by the word of Jehovah against the altar in Bethel, and against all the houses of the high places that are in the cities of Samaria, shall certainly come to pass.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: After this thing Jeroboam turned not from his evil way, but made again from all classes of the people priests of the high places: whosoever would, he consecrated him, and he became [one] of the priests of the high places.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And by this thing there was sin on the house of Jeroboam, even to cut it off and to destroy it from off the face of the earth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: At that time Abijah the son of Jeroboam was sick.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jeroboam said to his wife, Arise, I pray thee, and disguise thyself, that thou be not known to be the wife of Jeroboam; and go to Shiloh: behold, there is Ahijah the prophet, who told me that [I should be] king over this people.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And take with thee ten loaves, and cakes, and a cruse of honey, and go to him: he will tell thee what shall become of the lad.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jeroboam's wife did so, and arose and went to Shiloh, and came to the house of Ahijah. And Ahijah could not see; for his eyes were set by reason of his age.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah said to Ahijah, Behold, the wife of Jeroboam cometh to ask a thing of thee about her son; for he is sick: thus and thus shalt thou say unto her; for it shall be, when she cometh in, that she shall feign to be another.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it was so, when Ahijah heard the sound of her feet, as she came in at the door, that he said, Come in, thou wife of Jeroboam; why feignest thou to be another? But I am sent to thee with a hard [message].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Go, tell Jeroboam, Thus saith Jehovah theGod of Israel: Forasmuch as I exalted thee from among the people, and made thee prince over my people Israel,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and rent the kingdom away from the house of David, and gave it thee; and thou hast not been as my servant David, who kept my commandments, and who followed me with all his heart, to do only that which is right in my sight;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but thou hast done evil above all that were before thee, and hast gone and made thee othergods, and molten images, to provoke me to anger, and hast cast me behind thy back:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: therefore behold, I will bring evil upon the house of Jeroboam, and will cut off from Jeroboam every male, him that is shut up and left in Israel, and will take away the house of Jeroboam, as a man taketh away dung, till it be all gone.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Him that dieth of Jeroboam in the city shall the dogs eat, and him that dieth in the field shall the fowl of the heavens eat; for Jehovah hath spoken.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou, arise, go to thine own house; when thy feet enter into the city, the child shall die.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And all Israel shall mourn for him, and they shall bury him; for he only of Jeroboam shall come to the grave, because in him there is found something good toward Jehovah theGod of Israel, in the house of Jeroboam.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah shall raise up for himself a king over Israel, who shall cut off the house of Jeroboam that day; and what? ...even now.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah will smite Israel, as a reed is shaken in the water, and he will root up Israel out of this good land which he gave to their fathers, and will scatter them beyond the river, because they have made their Asherahs, provoking Jehovah to anger.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he will give Israel up because of the sins of Jeroboam, wherewith he has sinned, and made Israel to sin.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jeroboam's wife arose and departed, and came to Tirzah; when she came to the threshold of the door, the child died.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they buried him; and all Israel mourned for him, according to the word of Jehovah, which he spoke through his servant Ahijah the prophet.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the rest of the acts of Jeroboam, how he warred, and how he reigned, behold, they are written in the book of the chronicles of the kings of Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the days that Jeroboam reigned were twenty-two years; and he slept with his fathers. And Nadab his son reigned in his stead.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Rehoboam the son of Solomon reigned in Judah. Rehoboam was forty-one years old when he began to reign, and he reigned seventeen years in Jerusalem, the city that Jehovah had chosen out of all the tribes of Israel to put his name there; and his mother's name was Naamah, an Ammonitess.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Judah did evil in the sight of Jehovah, and they provoked him to jealousy with their sins which they committed more than all that their fathers had done.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they also built for themselves high places, and columns, and Asherahs on every high hill and under every green tree;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and there were also sodomites in the land. They did according to all the abominations of the nations that Jehovah had dispossessed before the children of Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass in the fifth year of king Rehoboam, [that] Shishak king of Egypt came up against Jerusalem.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he took away the treasures of the house of Jehovah, and the treasures of the king's house; he even took away all; and he took away all the shields of gold that Solomon had made.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And king Rehoboam made in their stead brazen shields, and committed them to the hands of the chief of the couriers who kept the entrance of the king's house.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it was so, that as often as the king entered into the house of Jehovah, the couriers bore them, and brought them again into the chamber of the couriers.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the rest of the acts of Rehoboam, and all that he did, are they not written in the book of the chronicles of the kings of Judah?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And there was war between Rehoboam and Jeroboam all [their] days.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Rehoboam slept with his fathers, and was buried with his fathers in the city of David. And his mother's name was Naamah, an Ammonitess. And Abijam his son reigned in his stead.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And in the eighteenth year of king Jeroboam the son of Nebat began Abijam to reign over Judah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He reigned three years in Jerusalem; and his mother's name was Maachah, a daughter of Abishalom.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he walked in all the sins of his father, which he had done before him; and his heart was not perfect with Jehovah hisGod, as the heart of David his father.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But for David's sake Jehovah hisGod gave him a lamp in Jerusalem, setting up his son after him, and establishing Jerusalem;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: because David did that which was right in the sight of Jehovah, and turned not aside from anything that he commanded him all the days of his life, except in the matter of Urijah the Hittite.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And there had been war between Rehoboam and Jeroboam all the days of his life.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the rest of the acts of Abijam, and all that he did, are they not written in the book of the chronicles of the kings of Judah? And there was war between Abijam and Jeroboam.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Abijam slept with his fathers; and they buried him in the city of David. And Asa his son reigned in his stead.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And in the twentieth year of Jeroboam king of Israel, Asa began to reign over Judah;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and he reigned forty-one years in Jerusalem; and his mother's name was Maachah, daughter of Abishalom.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Asa did what was right in the sight of Jehovah, as David his father.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he put away the sodomites out of the land, and removed all the idols that his father had made.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And also Maachah his mother he removed from being queen, because she had made an idol for the Asherah; and Asa cut down her idol, and burned it in the valley of Kidron.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But the high places were not removed; only, Asa's heart was perfect with Jehovah all his days.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he brought into the house of Jehovah the things which his father had dedicated, and the things which he himself had dedicated, silver and gold and vessels.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And there was war between Asa and Baasha king of Israel all their days.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Baasha king of Israel came up against Judah, and built Ramah, in order to let none go out or come in to Asa king of Judah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Asa took all the silver and the gold that were left in the treasures of the house of Jehovah, and in the treasures of the king's house, and gave them into the hand of his servants; and king Asa sent them to Ben-Hadad, the son of Tabrimmon, the son of Hezion, king of Syria, who dwelt at Damascus, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: There is a league between me and thee, [as] between my father and thy father; behold, I send thee a present of silver and gold: go, break thy league with Baasha king of Israel, that he may depart from me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Ben-Hadad hearkened to king Asa, and sent the captains of his forces against the cities of Israel, and smote Ijon, and Dan, and Abel-Beth-Maachah, and all Kinneroth, with all the land of Naphtali.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass when Baasha heard of it, that he left off building Ramah, and dwelt in Tirzah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And king Asa called together all Judah: none was exempted; and they carried away the stones and the timber from Ramah, with which Baasha had been building; and king Asa built with them Geba of Benjamin, and Mizpah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the rest of all the acts of Asa, and all his might, and all that he did, and the cities that he built, are they not written in the book of the chronicles of the kings of Judah? Only, in the time of his old age he was diseased in his feet.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Asa slept with his fathers, and was buried with his fathers in the city of David his father. And Jehoshaphat his son reigned in his stead.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Nadab the son of Jeroboam began to reign over Israel in the second year of Asa king of Judah; and he reigned over Israel two years.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he did evil in the sight of Jehovah, and walked in the way of his father, and in his sin with which he made Israel sin.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Baasha the son of Ahijah, of the house of Issachar, conspired against him; and Baasha smote him at Gibbethon, which [belonged] to the Philistines, when Nadab and all Israel were besieging Gibbethon.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Baasha slew him in the third year of Asa king of Judah, and reigned in his stead.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass when he was king, he smote all the house of Jeroboam; he left to Jeroboam none that breathed; until he had destroyed him, according to the word of Jehovah which he spoke by his servant Ahijah the Shilonite,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: because of the sins of Jeroboam which he sinned, and wherewith he made Israel to sin; by his provocation with which he provoked Jehovah theGod of Israel to anger.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the rest of the acts of Nadab, and all that he did, are they not written in the book of the chronicles of the kings of Israel?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And there was war between Asa and Baasha king of Israel all their days.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: In the third year of Asa king of Judah began Baasha the son of Ahijah to reign over all Israel in Tirzah, for twenty-four years.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he did evil in the sight of Jehovah, and walked in the way of Jeroboam, and in his sin where with he made Israel to sin.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the word of Jehovah came to Jehu the son of Hanani against Baasha, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Forasmuch as I exalted thee out of the dust, and made thee prince over my people Israel, and thou hast walked in the way of Jeroboam, and hast made my people Israel to sin, provoking me to anger with their sins;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: behold, I will take away Baasha and his house, and will make thy house like the house of Jeroboam the son of Nebat.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Him that dieth of Baasha in the city shall the dogs eat, and him that dieth of his in the field shall the fowl of the heavens eat.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the rest of the acts of Baasha, and what he did, and his might, are they not written in the book of the chronicles of the kings of Israel?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Baasha slept with his fathers, and was buried in Tirzah; and Elah his son reigned in his stead.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And also through the prophet Jehu the son of Hanani the word of Jehovah came against Baasha, and against his house, even for all the evil that he did in the sight of Jehovah, provoking him to anger with the work of his hands, in being like the house of Jeroboam; and because he had smitten him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: In the twenty-sixth year of Asa king of Judah, Elah the son of Baasha began to reign over Israel in Tirzah for two years.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And his servant Zimri, captain of half [his] chariots, conspired against him; and he was in Tirzah, drinking himself drunk in the house of Arza, who was the steward of his house in Tirzah;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and Zimri went in and smote him, and killed him, in the twenty-seventh year of Asa king of Judah, and he reigned in his stead.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass when he began to reign, as soon as he sat on his throne, he slew all the house of Baasha: he left him not a male, neither of his kinsmen nor of his friends.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Zimri destroyed all the house of Baasha, according to the word of Jehovah, which he spoke against Baasha through Jehu the prophet,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for all the sins of Baasha, and the sins of Elah his son, which they sinned and wherewith they made Israel to sin, provoking Jehovah theGod of Israel to anger with their vanities.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the rest of the acts of Elah, and all that he did, are they not written in the book of the chronicles of the kings of Israel?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: In the twenty-seventh year of Asa king of Judah, Zimri reigned seven days in Tirzah. Now the people were encamped against Gibbethon, which [belonged] to the Philistines.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the people that were encamped heard say, Zimri has conspired, and has also smitten the king; and all Israel made Omri, the captain of the host, king over Israel that day in the camp.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Omri went up from Gibbethon, and all Israel with him, and they besieged Tirzah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass when Zimri saw that the city was taken, that he went into the citadel of the king's house, and burned the king's house over him with fire;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and he died for his sins which he sinned in doing evil in the sight of Jehovah, in walking in the way of Jeroboam, and in his sin which he did, making Israel to sin.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the rest of the acts of Zimri, and his conspiracy which he wrought, are they not written in the book of the chronicles of the kings of Israel?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then were the people of Israel divided into two parts: half of the people followed Tibni the son of Ginath, to make him king; and half followed Omri.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But the people that followed Omri overcame the people that followed Tibni the son of Ginath; and Tibni died, and Omri reigned.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: In the thirty-first year of Asa king of Judah, Omri began to reign over Israel, — twelve years; he reigned six years in Tirzah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he bought the hill Samaria of Shemer for two talents of silver; and built on the hill, and called the name of the city that he built, after the name of Shemer, owner of the hill, Samaria.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Omri wrought evil in the sight of Jehovah, and did worse than all that were before him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he walked in all the way of Jeroboam the son of Nebat, and in his sins wherewith he made Israel to sin, provoking Jehovah theGod of Israel to anger with their vanities.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the rest of the acts of Omri, what he did, and his might which he shewed, are they not written in the book of the chronicles of the kings of Israel?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Omri slept with his fathers, and was buried in Samaria; and Ahab his son reigned in his stead.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Ahab the son of Omri began to reign over Israel in the thirty-eighth year of Asa king of Judah; and Ahab the son of Omri reigned over Israel in Samaria twenty-two years.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Ahab the son of Omri wrought evil in the sight of Jehovah more than all that were before him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass, as if it was a light thing for him to walk in the sins of Jeroboam the son of Nebat, that he took as wife Jezebel the daughter of Ethbaal king of the Zidonians; and he went and served Baal and worshipped him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he reared up an altar for Baal in the house of Baal, which he built in Samaria.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Ahab made the Asherah; and Ahab did more to provoke Jehovah theGod of Israel to anger than all the kings of Israel that were before him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: In his days Hiel the Bethelite built Jericho; he laid its foundation in Abiram his firstborn, and set up its gates in Segub his youngest, according to the word of Jehovah which he spoke through Joshua the son of Nun.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Elijah the Tishbite, of the inhabitants of Gilead, said to Ahab, As Jehovah theGod of Israel liveth, before whom I stand, there shall not be dew nor rain these years, except by my word.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the word of Jehovah came to him saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Get thee hence, and turn thee eastward, and hide thyself by the torrent Cherith, which is before the Jordan.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it shall be, that thou shalt drink of the torrent; and I have commanded the ravens to feed thee there.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he went and did according to the word of Jehovah; he went and abode by the torrent Cherith, which is before the Jordan.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the ravens brought him bread and flesh in the morning, and bread and flesh in the evening; and he drank of the torrent.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass after a while that the torrent dried up, for there had been no rain in the land.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the word of Jehovah came to him saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Arise, go to Zarephath, which is by Zidon, and abide there: behold, I have commanded a widow woman there to maintain thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he arose and went to Zarephath; and when he came to the entrance of the city, behold, a widow woman was there gathering sticks. And he called to her and said, Fetch me, I pray thee, a little water in a vessel, that I may drink.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And she went to fetch [it], and he called to her and said, Bring me, I pray thee, a morsel of bread in thy hand.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And she said, As Jehovah thyGod liveth, I have not a cake, but a handful of meal in a barrel, and a little oil in a cruse; and behold, I am gathering two sticks, that I may go in and dress it for me and my son, that we may eat it, and die.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Elijah said to her, Fear not; go, do as thou hast said; but make me thereof a little cake first; and bring it to me; and afterwards make for thee and for thy son.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For thus saith Jehovah theGod of Israel: The meal in the barrel shall not waste, neither shall the oil in the cruse fail, until the day that Jehovah sendeth rain upon the face of the earth!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And she went and did according to the word of Elijah; and she, and he, and her house, ate a whole year.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The meal in the barrel did not waste, neither did the oil in the cruse fail, according to the word of Jehovah which he had spoken through Elijah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass after these things, that the son of the woman, the mistress of the house, fell sick; and his sickness was so severe that there was no breath left in him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And she said to Elijah, What have I to do with thee, O thou man ofGod? art thou come to me to call mine iniquity to remembrance, and to slay my son?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to her, Give me thy son. And he took him out of her bosom, and carried him up into the upper chamber where he abode, and laid him upon his own bed.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he cried to Jehovah and said, Jehovah, myGod, hast thou also brought evil upon the widow with whom I sojourn, by slaying her son?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he stretched himself upon the child three times, and cried to Jehovah and said, Jehovah, myGod, I pray thee, let this child's soul come into him again!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah heard the voice of Elijah, and the soul of the child came into him again, and he lived.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Elijah took the child, and brought him down from the upper chamber into the house, and delivered him to his mother; and Elijah said, See, thy son lives.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the woman said to Elijah, Now by this I know that thou art a man ofGod, and that the word of Jehovah in thy mouth is truth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass after many days, that the word of Jehovah came to Elijah in the third year, saying, Go, shew thyself to Ahab; and I will send rain upon the face of the earth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Elijah went to shew himself to Ahab. And the famine was severe in Samaria.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Ahab called Obadiah, who was the steward of his house (now Obadiah feared Jehovah greatly;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and it was so, when Jezebel cut off the prophets of Jehovah, that Obadiah took a hundred prophets, and hid them by fifty in a cave, and maintained them with bread and water);

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and Ahab said to Obadiah, Go through the land, to all the fountains of water and to all the torrents, perhaps we may find grass to save the horses and the mules alive, so that we may not have to destroy some of [our] beasts.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they divided the land between them to pass through it: Ahab went one way by himself, and Obadiah went another way by himself.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And as Obadiah was on the way, behold, Elijah met him; and he knew him, and fell on his face, and said, Is it indeed thou, my lord Elijah?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to him, I [am he]: go, say to thy lord, Behold Elijah!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said, What have I sinned, that thou givest thy servant into the hand of Ahab, to put me to death?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: As Jehovah thyGod liveth, there is no nation or kingdom whither my lord has not sent to seek thee; and when they said, He is not [here], he took an oath of the kingdom or nation that they found thee not.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And now thou sayest, Go, say to thy lord, Behold Elijah!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it shall come to pass when I am gone from thee, that the Spirit of Jehovah shall carry thee whither I know not; and when I come and tell Ahab, and he cannot find thee, he will kill me; and I thy servant fear Jehovah from my youth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Was it not told my lord what I did when Jezebel slew the prophets of Jehovah, how I hid a hundred men of Jehovah's prophets by fifty in a cave, and maintained them with bread and water?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And now thou sayest, Go, say to thy lord, Behold Elijah! and he will kill me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Elijah said, As Jehovah of hosts liveth, before whom I stand, I will certainly shew myself to him to-day.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then Obadiah went to meet Ahab, and told him. And Ahab went to meet Elijah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass when Ahab saw Elijah, that Ahab said to him, Is it thou, the troubler of Israel?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said, I have not troubled Israel, but thou and thy father's house, in that ye have forsaken the commandments of Jehovah, and thou hast followed the Baals.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And now send, gather to me all Israel to mount Carmel, and the prophets of Baal four hundred and fifty, and the prophets of the Asherah four hundred, who eat at Jezebel's table.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: So Ahab sent to all the children of Israel, and gathered the prophets together unto mount Carmel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then Elijah drew near to all the people, and said, How long do ye halt between two opinions? if Jehovah beGod, follow him; and if Baal, follow him. And the people answered him not a word.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Elijah said to the people, I, only I, remain a prophet of Jehovah; and Baal's prophets are four hundred and fifty men.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let them therefore give us two bullocks: and let them choose one bullock for themselves, and cut it in pieces, and put it on the wood, and put no fire; and I will sacrifice the other bullock, and put it on the wood, and put no fire.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And call ye on the name of yourgods, and I will call on the name of Jehovah; and thegod that answers by fire, let him beGod. And all the people answered and said, The word is good.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Elijah said to the prophets of Baal, Choose one bullock for yourselves, and sacrifice it first; for ye are the many; and call on the name of yourgod, but put no fire.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they took the bullock which had been given them, and sacrificed it, and called on the name of Baal from morning until noon, saying, O Baal, answer us! But there was no voice, and none answered. And they leaped about the altar that had been made.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass at noon that Elijah mocked them and said, Cry aloud; for he is agod; for he is meditating, or gone aside, or he is on a journey; perhaps he sleeps, and will awake.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they cried aloud, and cut themselves after their manner with swords and spears, till the blood gushed out upon them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass when midday was past, that they prophesied until the [time] of the offering up of the oblation; but there was neither voice, nor any that answered, nor any attention.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then Elijah said to all the people, Draw near to me. And all the people drew near to him. And he repaired the altar of Jehovah which was broken down.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Elijah took twelve stones, according to the number of the tribes of the sons of Jacob, to whom the word of Jehovah came saying, Israel shall be thy name;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and with the stones he built an altar in the name of Jehovah, and made a trench round about the altar, of the capacity of two measures of seed;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and he put the wood in order, and cut the bullock in pieces, and laid it on the wood. And he said, Fill four pitchers with water, and pour it on the burnt-offering, and on the wood.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said, Do it the second time. And they did it the second time. And he said, Do it the third time. And they did it the third time.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the water ran round about the altar; and he filled the trench also with water.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass at [the time of] the offering up of the oblation, that Elijah the prophet drew near, and said, Jehovah,God of Abraham, Isaac and Israel, let it be known this day that thou artGod in Israel, and that I am thy servant, and that I have done all these things by thy word.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Answer me, Jehovah, answer me, that this people may know that thou Jehovah artGod, and [that] thou hast turned their heart back again.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the fire of Jehovah fell, and consumed the burnt-offering, and the wood, and the stones, and the dust, and licked up the water that was in the trench.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And all the people saw [it], and they fell on their faces and said, Jehovah, he isGod! Jehovah, he isGod!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Elijah said to them, Seize the prophets of Baal; let not one of them escape! And they seized them; and Elijah brought them down to the torrent of Kishon, and slaughtered them there.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Elijah said to Ahab, Go up, eat and drink; for there is a sound of abundance of rain.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Ahab went up to eat and to drink. And Elijah went up to the top of Carmel; and he bowed down on the earth, and put his face between his knees.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to his servant, Go up now, look toward the sea. And he went up and looked, and said, [There is] nothing. And he said, Go again seven times.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass at the seventh time that he said, Behold there is a cloud, small as a man's hand, arising out of the sea. And he said, Go up, say to Ahab, Harness and go down, that the pour of rain stop thee not.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass in the mean while, that the heavens became black [with] clouds and wind, and there was a great pour of rain. And Ahab got on the chariot, and went to Jizreel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the hand of Jehovah was upon Elijah; and he girded up his loins, and ran before Ahab to the entrance of Jizreel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Ahab told Jezebel all that Elijah had done, and in detail how he had slain all the prophets with the sword.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jezebel sent a messenger to Elijah saying, So do thegods [to me], and more also, if I make not thy life as the life of one of them by to-morrow about this time!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when he saw [that], he arose, and went for his life, and came to Beer-sheba, which belongs to Judah, and left his servant there.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he himself went a day's journey into the wilderness, and came and sat down under a certain broom-bush, and requested for himself that he might die; and said, It is enough: now, Jehovah, take my life; for I am not better than my fathers.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he lay down and slept under the broom-bush. And behold, an angel touched him, and said to him, Arise, eat!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he looked, and behold, at his head was a cake, baked on hot stones, and a cruse of water. And he ate and drank, and lay down again.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the angel of Jehovah came again the second time, and touched him, and said, Arise, eat; for the journey is too great for thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he arose, and ate and drank, and went in the strength of that food forty days and forty nights to Horeb the mount ofGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And there he went into a cave, and lodged there. And behold, the word of Jehovah [came] to him, and he said to him, What doest thou here, Elijah?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said, I have been very jealous for Jehovah theGod of hosts; for the children of Israel have forsaken thy covenant, thrown down thine altars, and slain thy prophets with the sword; and I am left, I alone, and they seek my life, to take it away.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said, Go forth, and stand upon the mount before Jehovah. And behold, Jehovah passed by, and a great and strong wind rent the mountains, and broke in pieces the rocks before Jehovah: Jehovah was not in the wind. And after the wind, an earthquake: Jehovah was not in the earthquake.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And after the earthquake, a fire: Jehovah was not in the fire. And after the fire, a soft gentle voice.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass, when Elijah heard it, that he wrapped his face in his mantle, and went out, and stood in the entrance of the cave. And behold, a voice [came] to him and said, What doest thou here, Elijah?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said, I have been very jealous for Jehovah theGod of hosts; for the children of Israel have forsaken thy covenant, thrown down thine altars, and slain thy prophets with the sword; and I am left, I alone, and they seek my life, to take it away.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah said to him, Go, return on thy way to the wilderness of Damascus; and when thou comest, anoint Hazael king over Syria;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and Jehu the son of Nimshi shalt thou anoint king over Israel; and Elisha the son of Shaphat of Abel-meholah shalt thou anoint prophet in thy stead.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it shall come to pass, [that] him that escapeth the sword of Hazael shall Jehu slay; and him that escapeth the sword of Jehu shall Elisha slay.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Yet I have left [myself] seven thousand in Israel, all the knees that have not bowed unto Baal, and every mouth that hath not kissed him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he departed thence, and found Elisha the son of Shaphat, who was ploughing [with] twelve yokes before him, and he with the twelfth; and Elijah went over to him, and cast his mantle on him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he left the oxen, and ran after Elijah, and said, Let me, I pray thee, kiss my father and my mother, and I will follow thee. And he said to him, Go back again; for what have I done to thee?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he returned back from him, and took the yoke of oxen, and killed them, and boiled their flesh with the implements of the oxen, and gave to the people, and they ate. And he arose and went after Elijah, and ministered to him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Ben-Hadad king of Syria assembled all his host; and there were thirty-two kings with him, and horses and chariots; and he went up and besieged Samaria, and fought against it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he sent messengers to Ahab king of Israel into the city, and said to him, Thus says Ben-Hadad:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thy silver and thy gold is mine; thy wives also and thy children, the goodliest, are mine.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the king of Israel answered and said, My lord, O king, according to thy saying, I am thine, and all that I have.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the messengers came again, and said, Thus speaks Ben-Hadad saying: I sent to thee indeed, saying, Thou shalt deliver me thy silver, and thy gold, and thy wives, and thy children;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but to-morrow about this time I will send my servants to thee, and they shall search thy house, and the houses of thy servants; and it shall be, that whatsoever is pleasant in thy sight, they shall put in their hand and take away.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the king of Israel called all the elders of the land and said, Mark, I pray you, and see how this [man] seeks mischief; for he sent to me for my wives, and for my children, and for my silver, and for my gold; and I denied him not.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And all the elders and all the people said to him, Hearken not, nor consent.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to the messengers of Ben-Hadad, Tell my lord the king, All that thou didst send for to thy servant at the first I will do; but this thing I cannot do. And the messengers departed, and brought him word again.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Ben-Hadad sent to him and said, Thegods do so to me, and more also, if the dust of Samaria shall suffice for handfuls for all the people that follow me!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the king of Israel answered and said, Tell [him], Let not him that girdeth on boast himself as he that putteth off!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass when he heard this word, as he was drinking, he and the kings in the tents, that he said to his servants, Set yourselves. And they set themselves against the city.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And behold, a prophet drew near to Ahab king of Israel, and said, Thus saith Jehovah: Hast thou seen all this great multitude? behold, I will deliver it into thy hand this day; and thou shalt know that I am Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Ahab said, By whom? And he said, Thus saith Jehovah: By the servants of the princes of the provinces. Then he said, Who shall begin the battle? And he said, Thou.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he numbered the servants of the princes of the provinces, and they were two hundred and thirty-two; and after them he numbered all the people, all the children of Israel, seven thousand.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they went out at noon; and Ben-Hadad drank himself drunk in the tents, he and the kings, the thirty-two kings that helped him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the servants of the princes of the provinces went out first; and Ben-Hadad sent out, and they told him saying, There are men come out of Samaria.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said, Whether they be come out for peace, take them alive; or whether they be come out for war, take them alive.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And these servants of the princes of the provinces came out of the city, and the army that followed them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they slew every one his man; and the Syrians fled, and Israel pursued them; and Ben-Hadad the king of Syria escaped on a horse with the horsemen.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the king of Israel went out, and smote the horses and chariots, and slew the Syrians with a great slaughter.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the prophet drew near to the king of Israel, and said to him, Go, strengthen thyself, and understand, and see what thou shalt do; for at the return of the year the king of Syria will come up against thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the servants of the king of Syria said to him, Theirgods aregods of the mountains; therefore they were stronger than we; but if we fight against them on the plateau, shall we not be stronger than they?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And do this: take the kings away, every man out of his place, and put governors in their stead;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and number thee an army, like the army that thou hast lost, horse for horse, and chariot for chariot; and we will fight against them on the plateau: shall we not be stronger than they? And he hearkened to their voice, and did so.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass, at the return of the year, that Ben-Hadad numbered the Syrians, and went up to Aphek, to fight against Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the children of Israel were numbered and victualled, and they went against them; and the children of Israel encamped before them like two little flocks of goats; but the Syrians filled the land.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the man ofGod drew near, and spoke to the king of Israel and said, Thus saith Jehovah: Because the Syrians have said, Jehovah is agod of the mountains, but he is not agod of the valleys, I will give all this great multitude into thy hand, and ye shall know that I am Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they encamped one over against the other seven days; and it came to pass that on the seventh day the battle was joined; and the children of Israel smote of the Syrians a hundred thousand footmen in one day.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the rest fled to Aphek, into the city; and the wall fell on twenty-seven thousand men of them that were left. And Ben-Hadad fled, and came into the city, [from] chamber to chamber.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And his servants said to him, Behold now, we have heard that the kings of the house of Israel are merciful kings: let us, I pray thee, put sackcloth on our loins, and ropes upon our heads, and go out to the king of Israel; perhaps he will save thy life.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they girded sackcloth on their loins, and ropes on their heads, and came to the king of Israel, and said, Thy servant Ben-Hadad says, I pray thee, let me live. And he said, Is he yet alive? he is my brother.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the men took it as a good omen, and hastened to catch what came from him, and they said, Thy brother Ben-Hadad. ...And he said, Go, bring him. And Ben-Hadad came forth to him; and he caused him to come up into the chariot.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And [Ben-Hadad] said to him, The cities that my father took from thy father I will restore; and thou shalt make streets for thyself in Damascus, as my father made in Samaria. And I [said Ahab] will send thee away with this covenant. So he made a covenant with him, and sent him away.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And a certain man of the sons of the prophets said to another by the word of Jehovah, Smite me, I pray thee. But the man refused to smite him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then said he to him, Because thou hast not hearkened to the voice of Jehovah, behold, when thou departest from me, the lion will slay thee. And when he had departed from him, the lion found him and slew him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then he found another man, and said, Smite me, I pray thee. And the man smote him violently, and wounded [him].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the prophet departed, and waited for the king by the way, and disguised himself with a sash over his eyes.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And as the king passed by, he cried to the king and said, Thy servant went out into the midst of the battle; and behold, a man turned aside, and brought a man to me and said, Keep this man; if by any means he be missing, then shall thy life be for his life, or thou shalt pay a talent of silver.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And as thy servant was busy here and there, he was gone. And the king of Israel said to him, So [is] thy judgment: thyself hast decided [it].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then he hastily took the sash away from his face; and the king of Israel discerned him, that he was of the prophets.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to him, Thus saith Jehovah: Because thou hast let go out of thy hand the man that I had devoted to destruction, thy life shall be for his life, and thy people for his people.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the king of Israel went to his house sullen and vexed, and came to Samaria.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass after these things, [that] Naboth the Jizreelite had a vineyard, which was in Jizreel, by the side of the palace of Ahab king of Samaria.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Ahab spoke to Naboth saying, Give me thy vineyard, that I may have it for a garden of herbs, for it is near, by the side of my house; and I will give thee for it a better vineyard than it; if it seem good to thee, I will give thee its value in money.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Naboth said to Ahab, Jehovah forbid it me, that I should give the inheritance of my fathers to thee!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Ahab came into his house sullen and vexed because of the word that Naboth the Jizreelite had spoken to him; for he had said, I will not give thee the inheritance of my fathers. And he lay down on his bed, and turned away his face, and ate no bread.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jezebel his wife came to him, and said to him, Why is thy spirit sullen, and thou eatest no bread?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to her, Because I spoke to Naboth the Jizreelite and said to him, Give me thy vineyard for money; or else, if it please thee, I will give thee a vineyard for it; and he said, I will not give thee my vineyard.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jezebel his wife said to him, Dost thou now exercise sovereignty over Israel? arise, eat bread, and let thy heart be glad: I will give thee the vineyard of Naboth the Jizreelite.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And she wrote a letter in Ahab's name, and sealed it with his seal, and sent the letter to the elders and to the nobles that were in his city, dwelling with Naboth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And she wrote in the letter saying, Proclaim a fast, and set Naboth at the head of the people;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and set two men, sons of Belial, before him, and they shall bear witness against him saying, Thou didst curseGod and the king; and carry him out, and stone him, that he may die.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the men of his city, the elders and the nobles that dwelt in his city, did as Jezebel had sent to them, as it was written in the letter that she had sent to them:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: they proclaimed a fast, and set Naboth at the head of the people.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And there came the two men, sons of Belial, and sat before him; and the men of Belial witnessed against him, against Naboth, in the presence of the people, saying, Naboth blasphemedGod and the king. And they carried him forth out of the city, and stoned him with stones, that he died.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they sent to Jezebel saying, Naboth is stoned, and is dead.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass when Jezebel heard that Naboth was stoned and was dead, that Jezebel said to Ahab, Arise, take possession of the vineyard of Naboth the Jizreelite, which he refused to give thee for money; for Naboth is not alive, but dead.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass when Ahab heard that Naboth was dead, that Ahab rose up to go down to the vineyard of Naboth the Jizreelite, to take possession of it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the word of Jehovah came to Elijah the Tishbite, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Arise, go down to meet Ahab king of Israel, who is in Samaria: behold, he is in the vineyard of Naboth, whither he is gone down to take possession of it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou shalt speak unto him saying, Thus saith Jehovah: Hast thou killed, and also taken possession? And thou shalt speak unto him saying, Thus saith Jehovah: In the place where the dogs licked the blood of Naboth shall the dogs lick thy blood, even thine.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Ahab said to Elijah, Hast thou found me, mine enemy? And he said, I have found [thee]; because thou hast sold thyself to do evil in the sight of Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Behold, I will bring evil upon thee, and will take away thy posterity, and will cut off from Ahab every male, and him that is shut up and left in Israel;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and I will make thy house like the house of Jeroboam the son of Nebat, and like the house of Baasha the son of Ahijah, for the provocation wherewith thou hast provoked me to anger, and made Israel to sin.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And of Jezebel also spoke Jehovah saying, The dogs shall eat Jezebel by the moat of Jizreel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Him that dieth of Ahab in the city shall the dogs eat, and him that dieth in the field shall the fowl of the heavens eat.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: (Surely there was none like to Ahab, who did sell himself to do evil in the sight of Jehovah, Jezebel his wife urging him on.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he did very abominably in following idols, according to all that the Amorites did, whom Jehovah had dispossessed before the children of Israel.)

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass when Ahab heard these words, that he rent his garments, and put sackcloth upon his flesh, and fasted, and lay in sackcloth, and went softly.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the word of Jehovah came to Elijah the Tishbite, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Seest thou how Ahab humbleth himself before me? because he humbleth himself before me, I will not bring the evil in his days: in his son's days will I bring the evil upon his house.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they continued three years without war between Syria and Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass in the third year, that Jehoshaphat the king of Judah came down to the king of Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the king of Israel said to his servants, Do ye know that Ramoth in Gilead is ours, and we keep quiet without taking it out of the hand of the king of Syria?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to Jehoshaphat, Wilt thou go with me to battle to Ramoth-Gilead? And Jehoshaphat said to the king of Israel, I am as thou, my people as thy people, my horses as thy horses.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehoshaphat said to the king of Israel, Inquire, I pray thee, this day of the word of Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the king of Israel assembled the prophets, about four hundred men, and said to them, Shall I go against Ramoth-Gilead to battle, or shall I forbear? And they said, Go up, and the Lord will give it into the king's hand.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Jehoshaphat said, Is there not here a prophet of Jehovah besides, that we might inquire of him?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the king of Israel said to Jehoshaphat, There is yet one man by whom we may inquire of Jehovah; but I hate him, for he prophesies no good concerning me, but evil: [it is] Micah the son of Imlah. And Jehoshaphat said, Let not the king say so.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then the king of Israel called a chamberlain, and said, Fetch quickly Micah the son of Imlah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the king of Israel and Jehoshaphat the king of Judah, having put on their robes, sat each on his throne, in the open place at the entrance of the gate of Samaria; and all the prophets prophesied before them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Zedekiah the son of Chenaanah made for himself horns of iron, and he said, Thus saith Jehovah: With these shalt thou push the Syrians, until thou have exterminated them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And all the prophets prophesied so, saying, Go up to Ramoth-Gilead, and prosper; for Jehovah will give it into the king's hand.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the messenger that went to call Micah spoke to him saying, Behold now, the words of the prophets [declare] good to the king with one assent: let thy word, I pray thee, be like the word of one of them, and speak good.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Micah said, As Jehovah liveth, even what Jehovah shall say to me, that will I speak.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he came to the king. And the king said to him, Micah, shall we go against Ramoth-Gilead to battle, or shall we forbear? And he said to him, Go up, and prosper; for Jehovah will give it into the hand of the king.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the king said to him, How many times shall I adjure thee that thou tell me nothing but truth in the name of Jehovah?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said, I saw all Israel scattered upon the mountains, as sheep that have no shepherd. And Jehovah said, These have no master: let them return every man to his house in peace.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the king of Israel said to Jehoshaphat, Did I not tell thee that he prophesies no good concerning me, but evil?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said, Hear therefore the word of Jehovah: I saw Jehovah sitting upon his throne, and all the host of heaven standing by him, on his right hand and on his left;

translations.name: Darby Bible
