translations.text: and Jehovah said, Who shall entice Ahab that he may go up and fall at Ramoth-Gilead? And one said after this manner, and another said after that manner.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And there came forth a spirit, and stood before Jehovah, and said, I will entice him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehovah said unto him, Wherewith? And he said, I will go forth, and will be a lying spirit in the mouth of all his prophets. And he said, Thou shalt entice [him], and also succeed: go forth, and do so.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And now, behold, Jehovah has put a lying spirit in the mouth of all these thy prophets, and Jehovah has spoken evil concerning thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then Zedekiah the son of Chenaanah went near, and smote Micah upon the cheek, and said, Where now went the Spirit of Jehovah from me to speak to thee?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Micah said, Behold, thou shalt see on that day when thou shalt go from chamber to chamber to hide thyself.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the king of Israel said, Take Micah and carry him back to Amon the governor of the city, and to Joash the king's son;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and thou shalt say, Thus says the king: Put this [man] in the prison, and feed him with bread of affliction and with water of affliction, until I come in peace.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Micah said, If thou return at all in peace, Jehovah has not spoken by me. And he said, Hearken, O peoples, all of you!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the king of Israel and Jehoshaphat the king of Judah went up to Ramoth-Gilead.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the king of Israel said to Jehoshaphat, I will disguise myself, and will enter into the battle; but put thou on thy robes. And the king of Israel disguised himself, and went into the battle.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the king of Syria commanded the thirty-two captains of his chariots saying, Fight neither with small nor great, but with the king of Israel only.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass when the captains of the chariots saw Jehoshaphat, that they said, Surely that is the king of Israel; and they turned against him to fight; and Jehoshaphat cried out.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass that when the captains of the chariots perceived that it was not the king of Israel, they turned back from pursuing him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And a man drew a bow at a venture, and smote the king of Israel between the fastenings and the corslet. And he said to his charioteer, Turn thy hand, and drive me out of the camp; for I am wounded.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the battle increased that day; and the king was stayed up in his chariot against the Syrians, and he died at even; and the blood of the wound ran out into the hollow of the chariot.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And there went the cry throughout the host at the going down of the sun, saying, Every man to his city, and every man to his own country!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the king died, and was brought to Samaria; and they buried the king in Samaria.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And one washed the chariot in the pool of Samaria; and the dogs licked his blood, where the harlots bathed: according to the word of Jehovah, which he had spoken.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the rest of the acts of Ahab, and all that he did, and the ivory house that he built, and all the cities that he built, are they not written in the book of the chronicles of the kings of Israel?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Ahab slept with his fathers; and Ahaziah his son reigned in his stead.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehoshaphat the son of Asa began to reign over Judah in the fourth year of Ahab king of Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehoshaphat was thirty-five years old when he began to reign; and he reigned twenty-five years in Jerusalem; and his mother's name was Azubah, the daughter of Shilhi.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he walked in all the way of Asa his father; he turned not aside from it, doing what was right in the sight of Jehovah. Only, the high places were not removed: the people offered and burned incense still on the high places.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehoshaphat was at peace with the king of Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the rest of the acts of Jehoshaphat, and his might which he shewed, and how he warred, are they not written in the book of the chronicles of the kings of Judah?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the remainder of the sodomites, which were left in the days of his father Asa, he put away from out of the land.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And there was no king in Edom: a deputy reigned.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehoshaphat made Tarshish-ships to go to Ophir for gold; but they went not, for the ships were broken at Ezion-geber.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then said Ahaziah the son of Ahab to Jehoshaphat, Let my servants go with thy servants in the ships. But Jehoshaphat would not.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jehoshaphat slept with his fathers, and was buried with his fathers in the city of David his father; and Jehoram his son reigned in his stead.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Ahaziah the son of Ahab began to reign over Israel in Samaria the seventeenth year of Jehoshaphat king of Judah; and he reigned two years over Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he did evil in the sight of Jehovah, and walked in the way of his father, and in the way of his mother, and in the way of Jeroboam the son of Nebat, who made Israel to sin.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he served Baal, and worshipped him, and provoked Jehovah theGod of Israel to anger, according to all that his father had done.
